title: planType case mismatch: all MRR calculations silently return $0
detail: Schema default is @default("basic") (lowercase) and seed files write lowercase ("premium", "basic"). PLAN_PRICING keys are UPPERCASE (BASIC/PREMIUM/ENTERPRISE). PLAN_PRICING["basic"] = undefined → || 0, so every school contributes $0 MRR. The not:'TRIAL' filter also never excludes lowercase 'trial' schools (inflating school count). calculateMRR, getMRRHistory and mrrByPlan are all broken simultaneously.
fix: Normalize at the lookup site: PLAN_PRICING[school.planType.toUpperCase() as keyof typeof PLAN_PRICING] || 0. Apply the same toUpperCase() to the not:'TRIAL' filter. Longer-term, normalize planType to uppercase on write in onboarding/tenant update actions and seed files so the DB is consistent.
verdictReason: Verified: schema @default("basic"), seeds write lowercase, PLAN_PRICING keys are uppercase — lookup always returns undefined → 0.

title: Hardcoded 'operator' string in audit log breaks accountability
detail: Both reviewReceipt (line 58) and uploadReceipt (line 119) write userId: "operator" (literal string) to AuditLog. requireOperator() at line 11-16 of operator-auth.ts explicitly returns { userId: session.user.id }, so the real DEVELOPER user ID is available but not captured. The return value is discarded with bare await requireOperator(). invoiceUpdateStatus in billing/actions.ts correctly captures const operator = await requireOperator() and uses operator.userId.
fix: In both functions, change `await requireOperator()` to `const operator = await requireOperator()`, then pass `userId: operator.userId` in the auditLog.create calls. For uploadReceipt, also switch from db.auditLog.create directly to logOperatorAudit() to get IP/UA logging for free.
verdictReason: Confirmed: requireOperator() returns { userId: session.user.id } (operator-auth.ts:15) but receipts/actions.ts discards the return value at lines 21 and 91, then hardcodes 'operator' at lines 58 and 119.

title: reviewReceipt and uploadReceipt skip requireNotImpersonating()
detail: invoiceUpdateStatus in billing/actions.ts (lines 38-39) calls both requireOperator() and requireNotImpersonating(). The receipts actions call only requireOperator(). An impersonating DEVELOPER can approve/reject receipts and create receipt records — approving a receipt directly sets invoice status to 'paid' — while impersonating a school admin.
fix: Add `await requireNotImpersonating()` immediately after `await requireOperator()` in both reviewReceipt and uploadReceipt, matching the pattern in invoiceUpdateStatus. requireNotImpersonating is already imported in billing/actions.ts so just add the import in receipts/actions.ts.
verdictReason: Confirmed: billing/actions.ts lines 38-39 call both guards; receipts/actions.ts lines 21 and 91 call only requireOperator(). requireNotImpersonating is not imported in receipts/actions.ts at all.

title: TenantDetail panel fetches silently 404 — wrong base path /operator/
detail: The detail sheet fires three client-side fetches to /operator/tenants/{id}/summary, /operator/tenants/{id}/billing, and /operator/tenants/{id}/invoices. No /operator/* route segment exists in the app router. The actual routes live at /[lang]/(saas-dashboard)/tenants/[tenantId]/summary|billing|invoices. Every fetch returns 404, silently caught, so owners/metrics/billing/invoices are always empty/null when the operator opens the detail sheet.
fix: Replace the /operator/ prefix with the correct path. Since these are Next.js route handlers under (saas-dashboard), the client-accessible URL is /en/tenants/{id}/summary etc. — inject lang from a context or cookie. Alternatively, replace all three fetches with a single server action that returns { owners, metrics, billing, invoices } in one call.
verdictReason: detail.tsx lines 71/81/85 confirmed fetching /operator/tenants/... which has no matching route handler; routes exist only at /[lang]/(saas-dashboard)/tenants/[tenantId]/...

title: Outstanding balance always $0 — billing route selects non-existent Invoice.amount field
detail: The billing route casts `db` as unknown to call invoice.findMany with `select: { amount: true }`. Invoice model (prisma/models/school.prisma:451-452) has amountDue and amountPaid — there is no `amount` column. Prisma silently returns undefined for the unknown field, so outstandingCents reduces to 0 for every tenant. The operator always sees $0.00 outstanding.
fix: Remove the unsafe cast. Use db.invoice.findMany({ where: { schoolId: tenantId, status: 'open' }, select: { amountDue: true, amountPaid: true } }) and compute outstanding as (i.amountDue - i.amountPaid). Also fix planType fallback on line 39: 'basic' is the correct default (matches DB), not 'BASIC'.
verdictReason: Billing route.ts confirmed: InvoiceRowLite typed as { amount: number | null }, select: { amount: true } via unsafe cast. Invoice model has amountDue/amountPaid only — amount column does not exist.

title: analysis.tsx plan counts always return 0 — queries use uppercase planType but DB stores lowercase
detail: getTenantStats() queries with planType: 'TRIAL', 'BASIC', 'PREMIUM', 'ENTERPRISE' (uppercase). The School model stores planType as lowercase: 'trial', 'basic', 'premium', 'enterprise' — confirmed by the schema @default('basic'), saas-marketing/actions.ts creating schools with planType: 'trial', and school/configuration/actions.ts using z.enum(['basic','premium','enterprise']). All four plan counts return 0, making the plan distribution cards show 0 schools for every tier.
fix: Change the four count queries to use lowercase values: db.school.count({ where: { planType: 'trial' } }) etc. Do NOT change to uppercase — the DB stores lowercase.
verdictReason: Finding 9 in the original set incorrectly proposed fixing config.ts to uppercase. The real bug is in analysis.tsx using uppercase strings against a lowercase-storing DB. The fix direction must be reversed — use lowercase in analysis.tsx queries. Confirmed by saas-marketing/actions.ts planType:'trial' and schema @default('basic').

title: getMRRHistory fires 6 redundant superset queries — consolidate to 1 + JS bucketing
detail: Each of the 6 months runs a findMany where createdAt < nextMonth. The month-5 query is a strict superset of all earlier months. Plus calculateMRR (called in the same Promise.all in content.tsx) fires 2 more overlapping findMany on the same table. Combined: 8 School table scans per analytics page load.
fix: Replace all 6 month queries with one: const allSchools = await db.school.findMany({ where: { isActive: true, planType: { not: 'TRIAL' } }, select: { planType: true, createdAt: true } }); then bucket by createdAt in JS for each month boundary. Pass the same allSchools array to calculateMRR so content.tsx can share one data load.
verdictReason: Code confirmed: 6 separate findMany calls (each a superset of the prior) run with Promise.all; calculateMRR adds 2 more overlapping queries on the same table.

title: Projected 12 Months metric overstates future revenue by up to 76%
detail: Formula: currentMRR * Math.pow(1 + growth/100, 12) * 12. This computes the 12th-month MRR (after 12 months of compounding) times 12, not the sum of the next 12 monthly payments. At 10% monthly growth the formula returns ~$37,661 vs. the correct 12-month revenue sum of ~$21,384 — a 76% inflation.
fix: Replace with the geometric sum: growth !== 0 ? currentMRR * ((Math.pow(1 + growth/100, 12) - 1) / (growth/100)) : currentMRR * 12. Also rename label to 'Next 12 Months Revenue' to distinguish from ARR (already shown as currentMRR * 12).
verdictReason: Verified exact formula at line 243-244: `currentMRR * Math.pow(1 + mrrData.growth / 100, 12) * 12` — computes month-12 MRR * 12, not the 12-payment sum.

title: getMRRHistory uses current isActive state — churned schools disappear from all historical months
detail: findMany filters isActive: true for all historical month buckets. A school that churned last month will appear in zero historical buckets, making all past MRR readings lower than actual. The MRR chart shows a fabricated history. Same issue applies to lastMonthSchools in calculateMRR (lines 41-54).
fix: For historical MRR accuracy, remove the isActive filter and instead use createdAt < nextMonth only (counts all schools that had been created by that time). For precise tracking, schema needs School.deactivatedAt (noted in code comments as TODO). Mark this as needing schema change for the precise fix.
verdictReason: Verified: line 117-119 includes isActive: true in historical month queries, meaning churned schools are excluded from all past buckets.

title: revalidatePath uses bare '/billing' — never invalidates localized cache
detail: All four revalidatePath calls use bare '/billing' and '/billing/receipts'. The billing pages live under /[lang]/(saas-dashboard)/billing which renders as /en/billing or /ar/billing. The bare path '/billing' does not match the [lang] prefix segment so the RSC/ISR cache for the rendered pages is never actually invalidated on invoice status or receipt changes.
fix: Replace with revalidatePath('/', 'layout') to revalidate the entire layout subtree, which is the standard pattern for cross-locale cache busting in Next.js App Router.
verdictReason: Confirmed: billing/actions.ts:58 and receipts/actions.ts:66-67,125-126 all use bare '/billing' paths. The route tree has a [lang] segment so these paths never match the rendered URLs.

title: Upload Receipt button is a dead UI element — ReceiptUpload never rendered
detail: receipts/content.tsx renders a <Button> at line 154 with hardcoded text 'Upload Receipt' and an Upload icon, but no onClick, no form, no dialog. The ReceiptUpload component in upload.tsx is fully implemented (FileUploader, form, server action wired) but is never imported anywhere. The button is purely decorative — clicking it does nothing.
fix: Import ReceiptUpload from './upload'. Wrap it in a Sheet or Dialog triggered by the Upload Receipt button. Pass the needed invoices prop (fetch open invoices alongside receipts data in getReceiptsData or a separate query) plus dictionary and lang.
verdictReason: Confirmed: receipts/content.tsx line 154 renders a bare <Button> with no handler. ReceiptUpload from upload.tsx is not imported in content.tsx. grep for billing/receipts/upload across all src files returns zero results.

title: getBillingStats fires 5 separate DB round-trips — consolidate with groupBy
detail: getBillingStats at lines 109-124 issues 5 parallel queries in Promise.all: db.invoice.count() all, db.invoice.count({status:paid}), db.invoice.count({status:open}), db.invoice.aggregate(_sum amountPaid where paid), db.receipt.count({status:pending}). The first three count queries can be collapsed into one db.invoice.groupBy({by:['status'], _count:{id:true}}) to reduce from 5 to 3 round-trips.
fix: Replace the three count() calls with: `const statusCounts = await db.invoice.groupBy({ by: ['status'], _count: { id: true } })`. Derive totalInvoices, paidInvoices, openInvoices from the resulting array using a statusCounts.find(c => c.status === 'paid')?._count.id ?? 0 pattern. Keep the aggregate and receipt count as-is.
verdictReason: Confirmed: content.tsx lines 109-124 show three separate count() calls (all, paid, open) that can be reduced to a single groupBy query.

title: paymentRate denominator includes void/draft/uncollectible invoices — misleading metric
detail: paymentRate = Math.round((paidInvoices / totalInvoices) * 100) at lines 132-133. totalInvoices is db.invoice.count() with no status filter — it includes draft, void, uncollectible invoices. A school with many voided invoices will appear to have a low payment rate even if all collectible invoices are paid.
fix: Compute collectibleInvoices = openInvoices + paidInvoices; then paymentRate = collectibleInvoices > 0 ? Math.round((paidInvoices / collectibleInvoices) * 100) : 0. This measures actual collection performance on invoices that were ever expected to be paid.
verdictReason: Confirmed: content.tsx line 117 counts all invoices with no where clause; line 132 uses this unconstrained count as denominator, inflating the denominator with void/draft/uncollectible invoices.

title: reviewReceipt overwrites amountPaid instead of accumulating — incorrect for partial payments
detail: When a receipt is approved, lines 44-51 do: db.invoice.update({ data: { status: 'paid', amountPaid: receipt.amount } }). This sets amountPaid to the single receipt amount, overwriting any prior amountPaid. A second receipt approval on the same invoice would erase the first payment record. Additionally, status is always set to 'paid' regardless of whether amountPaid meets amountDue.
fix: Fetch the invoice first, then use Prisma's atomic increment: `amountPaid: { increment: receipt.amount }`. Only set status to 'paid' when the resulting amountPaid >= amountDue: compute new total and conditionally set status. This handles partial and full payment scenarios correctly.
verdictReason: Confirmed: receipts/actions.ts lines 44-51 show a hard assignment `amountPaid: receipt.amount` (not increment) and unconditionally sets status: 'paid'. Multiple receipt approvals on the same invoice would overwrite rather than accumulate.

title: receipts/columns.tsx accepts no dictionary — all strings hardcoded English
detail: getReceiptColumns(lang: Locale) takes only a Locale, with no dictionary parameter. All column headers (School, Invoice, File, Amount, Status), filter option labels (Pending, Approved, Rejected), action button text (Approve, Reject), status badge labels, and the reviewed-at inline text are hardcoded English strings. Contrast with getInvoiceColumns which at least accepts an optional dictionary parameter.
fix: Add an optional `dictionary?` parameter to getReceiptColumns. Use dictionary?.operator?.billing?.receipts?.columns for all column header strings and filter options. Pass the dictionary from ReceiptsContent at line 216 where getReceiptColumns(lang) is called.
verdictReason: Confirmed: receipts/columns.tsx line 20 signature is `getReceiptColumns(lang: Locale)` only. Lines 27-193 contain all-hardcoded English strings with no dictionary fallback.

title: receipts/content.tsx has ~12 hardcoded English UI strings
detail: All four stat card titles and descriptions are hardcoded English at lines 165, 170, 177, 182, 188, 193, 200, 204-205. The h2 heading at line 147 and paragraph at line 149-152 use optional-chain fallbacks to hardcoded English ('Payment Receipts', 'Review and approve manual payment receipts'). The Upload Receipt button label at line 156 and the EmptyState title/description at lines 222-223 are also hardcoded. The dictionary prop is available but not used for any of these strings.
fix: Add the missing keys to dictionary.operator.billing.receipts (totalReceipts, allTime, pendingReview, awaitingApproval, approved, processed, approvalRate, uploadReceipt, noReceipts, noReceiptsDescription) and replace all hardcoded strings with dictionary lookups.
verdictReason: Confirmed: receipts/content.tsx lines 165-206 and lines 154-157, 222-223 contain numerous hardcoded English strings. The dictionary prop is in scope but unused for these strings.

title: Analytics 'Assignments' card counts SchoolAssignment (school scope) instead of catalog Assignment templates
detail: Line 52 calls db.schoolAssignment.count() and stores the result as totalAssignments. SchoolAssignment is the school-scoped model with schoolId; it represents concrete class assignments, not catalog templates. The catalog analytics page intends to show platform-wide catalog template counts. db.assignment.count() is the correct model. The wrong count is also included in totalContent at line 91, making the 'Total Content' card equally wrong.
fix: Replace db.schoolAssignment.count() with db.assignment.count() at line 52.
verdictReason: Directly verified: line 52 is db.schoolAssignment.count(), SchoolAssignment is a different model from the catalog Assignment.

title: TOCTOU in rejectProposal — status guard is outside transaction, enabling double-reject
detail: rejectProposal reads proposal status at line 332 with a plain db.proposal.findUnique, checks it at line 341, then updates at line 348 in a separate round-trip. Two concurrent operator clicks can both see status='SUBMITTED', both pass the guard, and both update to REJECTED with different rejectionReasons and reviewedAt timestamps. The approveProposal path correctly wraps read+write in a $transaction with an atomic status check; the reject path does not.
fix: Wrap lines 332-403 in a db.$transaction(async (tx) => { ... }), move the findUnique and proposal.update inside the transaction. Mirror the pattern used in approveProposal (lines 114-256).
verdictReason: Directly verified: rejectProposal does findUnique+update in two separate statements outside any transaction; approveProposal uses $transaction with status check inside.

title: TOCTOU in approveExam/rejectExam/approveExamTemplate/rejectExamTemplate — no status guard and existence check is outside transaction
detail: All four exam/template approve+reject actions do: findUnique (checks existence only, not approval status) then update in a separate statement. There is no check that the exam is in PENDING state before approving; an already-APPROVED exam can be approved again, resetting approvedAt. Two concurrent approvals of the same exam can both succeed because the read and write are separate statements.
fix: For each action, either (a) wrap in $transaction and verify approvalStatus==='PENDING' before writing, throwing if not, or (b) use db.exam.updateMany({ where: { id, approvalStatus: 'PENDING' } }) and check count===0 to detect stale state.
verdictReason: Directly verified all four functions: each does findUnique (existence-only) then separate update with no PENDING status guard.

title: Denormalized chapter/lesson count updates run outside a transaction — race condition corrupts counts
detail: createChapter, deleteChapter, createLesson, and deleteLesson all follow the pattern: (1) mutate the child row, then (2) re-count siblings, then (3) update the parent denormalized counter — three separate round trips with no transaction. If two operators create chapters concurrently, both read the same stale count and one write overwrites the other, leaving totalChapters permanently wrong. createLesson makes 5 sequential DB calls without a transaction: create → findUnique(chapter) → count(chapterLessons) → update(chapter) → count(subjectLessons) → update(subject). The reorderChapters/reorderLessons actions correctly use $transaction — the same pattern must be applied here.
fix: Wrap each mutation + count + update triple in a db.$transaction() lambda. Use tx.chapter.count / tx.subject.update inside the lambda. For createLesson, include the chapter lookup in the same transaction to eliminate the extra round trip.
verdictReason: All four functions confirmed at the cited lines — no transaction around the create→count→update sequence. reorderChapters correctly uses $transaction, making the omission in CRUD mutations a clear inconsistency.

title: S3 thumbnail upload succeeds before DB update — failure orphans the S3 object
detail: uploadCatalogThumbnail calls processAndUploadCatalogImage (S3 write) first at line 63, then updates the DB at lines 66-81. If the DB update throws (entity not found, connection error), the uploaded image is stranded in S3 with no DB reference and will never be cleaned up. deleteCatalogThumbnail correctly reads the DB first but deletes S3 (line 132) before nulling the DB (lines 136-151) — on retry after a partial failure, the S3 key is gone but the DB still holds it, causing a broken reference.
fix: For upload: verify entity exists with findUnique first, then upload to S3, then update DB inside try/catch — on DB failure call deleteCatalogImage(key) to clean up the S3 object. For delete: null the DB field first, then delete from S3, so a retry sees the DB already cleared.
verdictReason: Confirmed at the cited lines: S3 write (line 63) precedes DB update (lines 66-81) with no compensating cleanup on DB failure. deleteCatalogThumbnail also has the reversed order (S3 delete at 132 before DB null at 136-151).

title: bar-graph.tsx generates Math.random() chart data on every render
detail: generateSampleData() calls Math.random() 180 times (90 days × desktop + mobile). useMemo([data]) re-runs on every mount because saas-client.tsx passes no data prop. The operator sees invented page-view numbers with no placeholder label.
fix: Remove generateSampleData() and the Math.random() calls. Render an explicit empty state or zero-bar with 'No data yet' overlay when no real data prop is passed. Wire real data via a server action querying db activity, or remove the chart until real data exists.
verdictReason: generateSampleData() confirmed at lines 58-74 using Math.random(); saas-client.tsx line 73 passes no data prop so fabricated path always executes.

title: RecentSales shows five fictional personas as live operator sales
detail: salesData is a module-level constant with 5 hardcoded names (Olivia Martin, Jackson Lee, Isabella Nguyen, William Kim, Sofia Davis), external avatar URLs, and dollar amounts. CardDescription reads 'You made 265 sales this month.' — none of this reflects real platform data.
fix: Replace with a server-fetched list of the most recent Invoice rows (status='paid', cross-tenant, orderBy amountPaid desc, take 5), mapping to real school names and amounts. Until wired, render an explicit empty state.
verdictReason: salesData constant confirmed at lines 15-51 with hardcoded fictional names, emails, avatars, and amounts. CardDescription '265 sales' is hardcoded.

title: AreaGraph renders hardcoded static 2024 visitor data with fabricated trend text
detail: chartData is a module-level constant: January-June 2024 desktop/mobile visitor numbers (186, 305, 237, 73, 209, 214) with no connection to any real metric. Footer reads 'Trending up by 5.2% this month' and 'January - June 2024'. Title 'Area Chart - Stacked' and description 'Showing total visitors for the last 6 months' are unrelated to the platform.
fix: Wire to real monthly school-signup or user-registration counts aggregated by month (db.school.groupBy on createdAt month), or remove the chart. Replace hardcoded footer trend with a computed value. Rename title/description to reflect what metric is actually shown.
verdictReason: chartData static 2024 values confirmed at lines 25-32; hardcoded trend text '5.2%' and 'January - June 2024' confirmed at lines 129-133.

title: PieGraph shows hardcoded browser-share data instead of plan distribution
detail: chartData contains five browser slices (Chrome 275, Safari 200, Firefox 287, Edge 173, Other 190) — a shadcn demo dataset. Footer reads 'Chrome leads with 25.4%' and 'Based on data from January - June 2024'. The TODO comment confirms replacement with plan distribution was planned but never done. Inner donut label says 'Total Visitors'.
fix: Query db.school.groupBy on a plan field to render real plan distribution. Until real schema supports plan tiers, render an empty state rather than browser market share data.
verdictReason: Browser-share chartData confirmed at lines 26-32; TODO comment at line 6 confirms this is known scaffolding. Footer 'January - June 2024' and 'Chrome leads' confirmed at lines 156-163.

title: SaasUpcoming displays hardcoded default counts — real platform data never fetched
detail: SaasUpcoming accepts optional props with defaults trialExpirations=3, pendingDomains=2, activeAlerts=1, upcomingRenewals=5. saas-client.tsx line 51 renders <SaasUpcoming locale={locale} /> passing no data props. The card always shows '1 active alert' badge and 'Trial Expirations: 3, Pending Domains: 2' even when there are no real alerts.
fix: Fetch real counts in content.tsx (parallel with existing Promise.all): count schools with isActive=false for trialExpirations; derive activeAlerts from real monitoring. Pass fetched values to SaasDashboardClient and forward to SaasUpcoming.
verdictReason: Default prop values confirmed at lines 33-36; saas-client.tsx line 51 confirmed passing only locale with no data props, so hardcoded defaults always render.

title: InvoiceHistorySection role=DEVELOPER returns empty then shows hardcoded 'License Revenue' defaults
detail: getInvoicesByRole for DEVELOPER falls through to getAllSchoolInvoices(schoolId) at actions.ts:3715, but schoolId is null for DEVELOPER and the guard at line 3699 returns [] immediately. The client component starts with hardcoded DEVELOPER defaults (four fictional rows: 'Enterprise - School A $2,499.00', 'Pro - School B $1,999.00'), receives [], condition data.length > 0 fails, and the hardcoded defaults remain displayed as 'License Revenue'.
fix: Add a dedicated getDeveloperInvoices() function in saas-dashboard that queries db.invoice cross-tenant (no schoolId filter), guarded by requireOperator(). Bypass school-scoped getInvoicesByRole entirely for the operator dashboard.
verdictReason: actions.ts line 3699 guard `if (!schoolId) return []` confirmed; DEVELOPER defaults at lines 289-318 confirmed as fictional invoice rows; condition `data.length > 0` at invoice-history-section.tsx line 367 confirmed.

title: ChartSection role=DEVELOPER renders hardcoded July-December growth series as 'Platform Analytics'
detail: defaultDataByRole.DEVELOPER has hardcoded July-December area chart data (35 schools/12000 users through 52/28000), hardcoded radial value=99 (Uptime), and generateBarChartData(3) deterministic arithmetic. Presented as 'Platform Analytics' with trend '+15.2%' and 'Platform availability: 99'.
fix: For DEVELOPER role, query real monthly school and user counts (db.school.groupBy by createdAt month for last 6 months) to build the area chart series. For the radial uptime, show 'N/A' or omit until a real monitoring source exists.
verdictReason: Hardcoded July-December data confirmed at lines 364-372; radial value=99 confirmed at line 354; DEVELOPER block confirmed at line 344 with title 'Platform Analytics'.

title: /operator/overview/metrics route does not exist — delta badges always show +0.0%
detail: MetricsCards fetches /operator/overview/metrics?period=7d. This route does not exist in the codebase (no file under src/app matching this path). The fetch returns a non-ok response, setDeltas is never called, all four KPI cards permanently show undefined delta rendered as +0.0%.
fix: Create the missing API route at src/app/api/operator/metrics/route.ts guarded by requireOperator(). Compute period-over-period deltas for school/user/student counts. Update fetch URL in metrics-cards.tsx to match the real route path.
verdictReason: No file matching /operator/overview/metrics found anywhere in src/app; fetch call at line 31 confirmed pointing to this non-existent route.

title: Kanban and Products are live in production nav despite being demo-only scaffolds
detail: Both 'Kanban' (href: /kanban) and 'Products' (href: /product) are confirmed in platformNav (lines 18-19) and also in saas-config.ts navigation entries (id='kanban', id='products') and actions (id='new-product'). Neither has DB backing. Kanban stores tasks in localStorage only; Products renders an empty table placeholder. No 'demo' label is shown.
fix: Remove both entries from src/components/template/saas-sidebar/config.ts lines 18-19 and from src/components/atom/generic-command-menu/saas-config.ts (kanban navigation entry, products navigation entry, new-product action). Keep route files but render an explicit Coming Soon page so direct navigation doesn't 404.
verdictReason: Both entries verified in config.ts lines 18-19 and saas-config.ts; features have no DB backing and no demo label.

title: ProductForm onSubmit is a no-op — submitting the form silently does nothing
detail: Confirmed: line 86 is literally `function onSubmit(_: z.infer<typeof formSchema>) {}`. The form wires a real FileUploader (lines 106-113) with onUploadComplete that calls form.setValue — meaning a file can be uploaded to S3 while the form data goes nowhere. Zero feedback to the user on submit.
fix: Short term: disable the submit button and add a visible 'Coming soon' notice or redirect /product to a not-yet-available page. Long term: implement a real server action or remove the form if the product concept is dropped.
verdictReason: Line 86 confirmed as empty function body; FileUploader is wired and can upload real files while form result is silently discarded.

title: Kanban localStorage store seeds two hardcoded demo tasks visible to the operator on first load
detail: Confirmed: initialTasks (lines 29-40) contains 'Project initiation and planning' and 'Gather requirements from stakeholders'. These pre-populate the kanban board on a clean browser. Because the store uses zustand persist (name: 'task-store', line 91) these demo tasks are permanently retained per browser after first visit. No cross-device or cross-user persistence; the operator cannot tell these are scaffolding rather than platform work items.
fix: Replace initialTasks with an empty array []. Render a visible empty-state component pointing to instructions if a getting-started guide is desired.
verdictReason: Lines 29-40 confirmed; zustand persist retains demo tasks permanently per browser with no labelling.

title: product/content.tsx 'Add New' href navigates to /dashboard/product/new — a 404; cell-action.tsx has same bug
detail: Confirmed: content.tsx line 32 has href='/dashboard/product/new' — a hard-coded, locale-less, non-existent path. The actual route is /[lang]/product/new. The same incorrect pattern appears in products/components/product-tables/cell-action.tsx line 59: router.push(`/dashboard/product/${data.id}`). The use-breadcrumbs.tsx reference to '/dashboard/product' at lines 26-28 is a pathname lookup key (not a navigation href) so it won't cause a 404 but will silently fail to match the real route /en/product.
fix: In content.tsx replace href='/dashboard/product/new' with href={`/${props.lang}/product/new`}. In cell-action.tsx, pass lang as a prop and use router.push(`/${lang}/product/${data.id}`). Update use-breadcrumbs.tsx key from '/dashboard/product' to '/{lang}/product' or use pathname-relative matching.
verdictReason: Core 404 finding in content.tsx and cell-action.tsx confirmed; use-breadcrumbs claim adjusted — it is a lookup key mismatch, not a navigation 404, but still a bug causing breadcrumbs to fail on the products route.

title: getDomainStats fires 5 separate COUNT queries — replace with 1 groupBy
detail: getDomainStats() issues 5 db.domainRequest.count() calls wrapped in Promise.all — already parallel, but still 5 separate DB round-trips. Prisma groupBy on the status field returns all buckets in one query.
fix: Replace with: const groups = await db.domainRequest.groupBy({ by: ['status'], _count: { _all: true } }); then fold into a status→count map. Reduces 5 DB round-trips to 1.
verdictReason: Verified at content.tsx lines 97-110: five separate db.domainRequest.count() calls inside Promise.all.

title: revalidatePath('/domains') never matches any route — staleness after every mutation
detail: All four mutating actions call revalidatePath('/domains'). The actual filesystem route is /[lang]/(saas-dashboard)/domains/page.tsx, so Next.js cache keys are /en/domains and /ar/domains. The path '/domains' does not match, so operators see stale data after every mutation.
fix: Enumerate supported locales: for (const l of ['en', 'ar']) { revalidatePath(`/${l}/domains`) }. The same systemic issue affects tenants/actions.ts and catalog actions — those should be fixed too.
verdictReason: Verified: actions.ts has four revalidatePath('/domains') calls; actual page is at src/app/[lang]/(saas-dashboard)/domains/page.tsx requiring /en/domains or /ar/domains.

title: domainVerify has a TOCTOU race: read→DNS check→write without status guard on the update
detail: domainVerify reads the DomainRequest (findUnique, line 152), then awaits verifyDNSRecords() — an external DNS call that can block for up to 30s — then updates to 'verified' (line 168) with no status guard in the where clause. A concurrent reject in that window silently overwrites the rejected status to verified.
fix: Add status: 'approved' to the update's where clause so a stale overwrite results in a 0-row update detectable as an error: db.domainRequest.update({ where: { id: validated.id, status: 'approved' }, data: { status: 'verified', verifiedAt: new Date() } }). Check affected count and return error if 0.
verdictReason: Verified: actions.ts line 152 findUnique, line 165 async DNS call, line 168 update with only { id: validated.id } in where — no status guard.

title: domainCreate silently throws on duplicate-pending domain — P2002 unhandled
detail: The guard (line 216) checks status IN ['approved','verified'] only. DomainRequest has @@unique([domain]) (domain.prisma line 15), so submitting the same domain with a 'pending' or 'rejected' record already present hits the DB unique constraint. Prisma throws PrismaClientKnownRequestError P2002, which the catch wraps as a generic 'Failed to create domain request' — no actionable user message. Two concurrent creates for the same domain can also both pass the findFirst guard before either commits.
fix: Extend the where clause to status: { in: ['approved', 'verified', 'pending'] }. Catch Prisma P2002 specifically and return errorCode: 'DOMAIN_ALREADY_EXISTS'. The DB unique constraint handles the race; map P2002 to the friendly error instead of a generic fallback.
verdictReason: Verified: domain.prisma line 15 has @@unique([domain]); actions.ts guard at line 219 only excludes ['approved','verified']; catch block at line 250 returns generic error with no P2002 handling.

title: Load-more in DomainsTable drops active filters and search on subsequent pages
detail: handleLoadMore calls getDomains({ page: nextPage, perPage }) without forwarding status filter or search term. DomainsTable receives no filter/search props from content.tsx. Filtering to 'pending' then loading more returns all-status results, silently merging wrong rows into the displayed list.
fix: Accept currentStatus and currentSearch as props on DomainsTable. Include them in every getDomains() call: getDomains({ page: nextPage, perPage, status: currentStatus, search: currentSearch }). Pass the values from content.tsx searchParams.
verdictReason: Verified: table.tsx line 48 calls getDomains({ page: nextPage, perPage }) only; DomainsTableProps (line 22-27) has no status or search fields; content.tsx passes no filter props at lines 236-241.

title: provider.ts, all.tsx, featured.tsx, card.tsx, use-observability.ts — entire component layer is dead code never imported by any production route
detail: The live observability page imports only ObservabilityContent from content.tsx, which queries the DB directly. provider.ts (fetchLogs, UnifiedLog type), all.tsx (AllLogs), featured.tsx (FeaturedLogs, MetricsOverview, ErrorLogs, ObservabilityDashboard), card.tsx (LogCard, MetricCard, HealthStatusCard), and hooks/use-observability.ts (useObservabilityData + /api/observability fetch) are exported but have zero production import sites. The /api/observability route does not exist. This dead layer adds ~700 lines of surface area and a duplicate conflicting UnifiedLog type.
fix: Delete provider.ts, all.tsx, featured.tsx, card.tsx (the observability-specific one), hooks/use-observability.ts, and util.ts functions that are only used by these dead files. Verify no barrel index re-exports them before deletion.
verdictReason: Grep confirms zero production import sites; fetchLogs only appears in its own definition; /api/observability route file does not exist anywhere in src/app/api.

title: Server-computed total row count is never passed to AuditLogTable — server-side pagination is non-functional
detail: content.tsx sets total = count from a parallel db.auditLog.count() call at line 85, but never passes it to AuditLogTable at line 103. AuditLogTable passes pageCount: -1 to useDataTable (table.tsx:25). useDataTable already sets manualPagination: true and uses nuqs URL state for pagination, but TanStack Table with pageCount:-1 cannot render accurate page controls. The operator only ever sees page 1 of audit logs with no indication that more pages exist.
fix: Add total and pageSize props to AuditLogTable and thread them through: pageCount: Math.ceil(total / pageSize). Call site in content.tsx: <AuditLogTable data={rows} columns={...} total={total} pageSize={limit} />. The useDataTable hook already accepts pageCount as a parameter.
verdictReason: content.tsx line 85 sets total = count; line 103 passes only data and columns to AuditLogTable; table.tsx line 25 hardcodes pageCount: -1.

title: Level and RequestId columns rendered in UI are always undefined — DB schema has neither field
detail: The AuditLog Prisma model (prisma/models/audit.prisma) has no level or requestId columns. content.tsx row mapping (lines 86-94) does not include these fields, so AuditRow.level and AuditRow.requestId are always undefined. columns.tsx (lines 60-87) renders Level and RequestId columns including a level select-filter with info/warn/error options. The operator can filter by level but the filter will never match any row. This is a silent correctness bug.
fix: Short-term: remove the level and requestId column definitions from getAuditColumns() and drop the level select-filter options. Long-term (needs schema): add level String? @default('info') and requestId String? to AuditLog via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS and populate them in the audit-write path.
verdictReason: AuditLog prisma model has no level or requestId fields; content.tsx row mapping omits both; columns.tsx defines select-filter on level that can never match.

title: TOCTOU race: update/delete ops check schoolId on find but not on write
detail: updateOperatorLead (line 131) and deleteOperatorLead (line 225) do a findFirst scoped to PLATFORM_SCHOOL_ID, then call db.lead.update/delete with where:{id} only. The logOperatorActivity bump at line 510 also uses where:{id:leadId} without schoolId. If a lead id were somehow reused across schoolIds (unlikely with cuid but the pattern is wrong), the write could target the wrong record.
fix: Replace db.lead.update({where:{id}}) with db.lead.updateMany({where:{id, schoolId:PLATFORM_SCHOOL_ID}}) and db.lead.delete with db.lead.deleteMany. Note: Prisma update()/delete() only accept unique fields in where — compound non-unique where requires updateMany/deleteMany. Apply same fix to the lead bump in logOperatorActivity at line 510.
verdictReason: Finding is real and confirmed; fix in original submission incorrectly states Prisma 6 supports compound non-unique where for update/delete — it does not; the correct fix uses updateMany/deleteMany.

title: Correctness: pilots/proposals monthly metric counts edits, not status transitions
detail: db.lead.count({ where: { status:'PROPOSAL', updatedAt:{ gte: monthStart } } }) counts all leads currently in PROPOSAL status that were touched this month — including leads that entered PROPOSAL in a prior month but were tagged, scored, or otherwise edited in the current month. A January PROPOSAL lead that gets a tag added in June reappears in June's count.
fix: Short-term: query LeadActivity where type='status_change' AND description contains 'PROPOSAL'/'CLOSED_WON' AND createdAt >= monthStart to count genuine transitions. Long-term: add statusChangedAt DateTime? to Lead and stamp on every status update (schema change, effort L).
verdictReason: Code at lines 102-108 confirmed using updatedAt for status-transition counting; comment at line 100 even notes this is an approximation but doesn't flag the semantic gap.

title: Perf: CSV bulk import fires N serial server-action round-trips
detail: onImport iterates over parsed leads in a for loop calling await createOperatorLead(...) sequentially for each row. A 50-row CSV fires 50 sequential server-action calls, each running requireOperator() + duplicate-email check + INSERT. Extremely slow at any batch size and hammers the Neon connection pool.
fix: Add a bulkCreateOperatorLeads(rows[]) server action: one requireOperator() check, then db.lead.createMany({ data: rows, skipDuplicates: true }). Return { imported, skipped } summary. Show a single progress indicator instead of per-row setProgress updates.
verdictReason: Code at lines 147-181 confirmed: for loop with sequential await createOperatorLead() calls; setProgress inside the loop also causes N re-renders.

title: Impersonation banner always invisible: impersonate_schoolId is httpOnly, hint cookies never set
detail: tenants/actions.ts:232 sets impersonate_schoolId with httpOnly:true — document.cookie cannot read it (line 22-25). The impersonate_hint and impersonate_expires cookies the banner also reads (lines 26-40) are never written anywhere in the codebase (confirmed by grep). Result: banner renders null 100% of the time. Operators have no visual indication they are impersonating.
fix: In tenantStartImpersonation, set a companion non-httpOnly cookie: cookieStore.set('impersonate_hint', JSON.stringify({ name: school.name }), { httpOnly: false, ... }) and cookieStore.set('impersonate_expires', expiresAt.toString(), { httpOnly: false, ... }). The auth-sensitive schoolId stays httpOnly; the banner reads only the display-safe hint.
verdictReason: Verified: impersonate_schoolId set httpOnly:true (tenants/actions.ts:232); grep finds zero writes of impersonate_hint or impersonate_expires anywhere; banner always returns null.

title: getMRRHistory fires 6 parallel DB queries (full table scan per month); getRevenueTrends fires 6 more aggregates
detail: getMRRHistory wraps 6 findMany calls in Promise.all (parallel, not sequential, but still 6 round-trips, each fetching all schools created before that month). getRevenueTrends similarly fires 6 db.invoice.aggregate calls in Promise.all. Combined with calculateMRR (2 queries), the analytics page makes 14 DB round-trips on load. The description of 'sequential' is inaccurate — they run in parallel — but the fan-out is still wasteful and will grow as months accumulate.
fix: Replace getMRRHistory's 6 findMany with one db.school.findMany({ where: { isActive: true, planType: { not: 'TRIAL' } }, select: { planType: true, createdAt: true } }) then bucket by month in JS. Replace getRevenueTrends's 6 aggregates with one db.invoice.findMany({ where: { status: 'paid' }, select: { amountPaid: true, createdAt: true } }) and sum by month in JS.
verdictReason: Real perf issue confirmed — 6+6 DB queries on page load. Finding description overstated 'sequential' but they use Promise.all; the fix (single query + JS bucketing) is correct and safe.

title: Fabricated data: overview.tsx shows hardcoded KPIs and Math.random chart data; BarGraph falls back to Math.random() when no data prop
detail: OverViewPage renders $1,250.00 total revenue, 1,234 new customers, 45,678 active accounts, 4.5% growth (all hardcoded). bar-graph.tsx:68-69 uses Math.random() in generateSampleData() and falls back to it when no data prop is passed (line 96). overview.tsx calls <BarGraph /> without data prop (line 137). OverViewPage is NOT reachable from any production route (dashboard/page.tsx imports DashboardContent instead), but the file and bar-graph.tsx fallback remain as credibility traps.
fix: Delete overview.tsx (confirmed orphan). In bar-graph.tsx, remove generateSampleData() and make data a required prop — any caller that omits data will get a TypeScript error instead of silently showing random numbers.
verdictReason: Verified: OverViewPage has hardcoded values; bar-graph.tsx:68-69 uses Math.random(); no production route imports OverViewPage; bar-graph.tsx has a TODO(production) comment acknowledging the issue.

title: OperatorAuthGuard is dead code with PII console.log on every render
detail: OperatorAuthGuard is exported but never imported or mounted anywhere (confirmed by grep). It logs userId, userRole, userEmail, and fullUser to the browser console on every render (lines 23-31), including after the component is conditionally returned null. If ever wired up, it leaks user PII to the browser console in production.
fix: Delete the file. The server-side auth check in the saas-dashboard layout is sufficient. If a client-side guard is needed, never log session user data to console.
verdictReason: Confirmed: grep finds zero imports of OperatorAuthGuard outside its own file; console.log at lines 23-31 logs userId/userRole/userEmail/fullUser unconditionally.

title: CSV export vulnerable to formula injection (school name, domain, invoice fields)
detail: getInvoicesCSV wraps cells in double-quotes (line 241) but does not strip or escape leading formula characters (=, +, -, @). A school name like =cmd|'/C calc'!A0 is exported verbatim. Fields at risk: school.name, school.domain, invoice.stripeInvoiceId — all operator/school-writable.
fix: Add sanitizeCsvCell: const sanitizeCsvCell = (v: string) => { const s = v.replace(/"/g, '""'); return /^[=+\-@\t\r]/.test(s) ? `'${s}` : s; } and apply it to every cell before joining.
verdictReason: Confirmed at line 241: cells are wrapped in double-quotes only, no formula-character stripping. school.name and stripeInvoiceId are operator-controllable inputs.

title: Audit log writes literal string 'operator' as userId instead of actual user ID
detail: reviewReceipt calls await requireOperator() without capturing the return value (line 21), so the userId is discarded. auditLog.create at line 58 writes userId: 'operator' (hardcoded string). Same pattern in uploadReceipt at line 119. requireOperator() returns { userId: session.user.id } — the real DEVELOPER user ID is available but ignored.
fix: Change line 21 to: const { userId } = await requireOperator() and use userId in both auditLog.create calls (lines 58 and 119).
verdictReason: Confirmed: requireOperator() at line 21 return value is discarded; literal string 'operator' at lines 58 and 119; requireOperator() does return { userId } per operator-auth.ts:15.

title: content.tsx has uncached local getTenants, bypassing queries.ts unstable_cache
detail: content.tsx declares a private async getTenants() that hits db.school.findMany + db.school.count directly with no caching. queries.ts exports a getTenants() wrapped in unstable_cache with 5s revalidation. The content.tsx version is called on every SSR render, firing two uncached DB round-trips. The queries.ts version is only used by the fetchTenants server action for client-side pagination.
fix: Delete the local getTenants in content.tsx. Refactor queries.ts getTenants to accept the same filter shape (nameEn, preferredLanguage in select for translation) and import it into content.tsx so the server-rendered initial view is cache-backed. Keep the getText translation loop separate.
verdictReason: content.tsx confirmed: local getTenants function with no cache wrapping. queries.ts exports a cached version. The two implementations are independent — content.tsx never imports from queries.ts.

title: N+1 translation: getText() called per tenant in a Promise.all loop
detail: content.tsx uses Promise.all(tenants.map(t => getText(...))). Translation rules (translation/CLAUDE.md) explicitly prohibit getText inside .map()/loops — each call is an LRU check + potential DB hit + potential Google Translate API call. With N tenants per page (up to 100), this is N parallel translation resolutions. The short-circuit for nameEn helps for /en views, but any Arabic-named school on an /en page still hits getText per row.
fix: Replace with a batched getLabels(tenants.map(t => t.name), lang, tenantId) call, which deduplicates and batches. Alternatively register School.name in translation/registry.ts TRANSLATABLE map to enable localize().
verdictReason: content.tsx lines 88-97 confirmed: Promise.all wrapping tenants.map(...getText...). Translation rules confirm getText must never be used inside loops.

title: analysis.tsx fires 10 sequential/parallel DB round-trips, reducible to ~4 with groupBy
detail: getTenantStats() runs 9 parallel counts (totalTenants, activeTenants, inactiveTenants, trialTenants, basicTenants, premiumTenants, enterpriseTenants, totalStudents, totalTeachers) and then 1 sequential count for recentSignups. The 7 school-count queries can be replaced by 2 groupBy queries on planType and isActive. The recentSignups should be moved into the Promise.all to remove the sequential round-trip.
fix: Use db.school.groupBy({ by: ['planType'], _count: { _all: true } }) and db.school.groupBy({ by: ['isActive'], _count: { _all: true } }) inside a single Promise.all with student/teacher/recentSignups counts. Total: 10 queries → 4. Note: groupBy values must be lowercase ('trial', 'basic', etc.) to match DB storage.
verdictReason: analysis.tsx lines 36-54 confirmed: 9 counts in Promise.all followed by await db.school.count() for recentSignups outside the Promise.all — sequential extra round-trip confirmed.

title: getRevenueTrends endDate misses invoices created during final hours of each month
detail: endDate = new Date(now.getFullYear(), now.getMonth() - i + 1, 0) = day 0 of next month = last day at 00:00:00 local time. With lte: endDate, any invoice created after midnight local time on the last day falls in neither this month nor next month in UTC+offset environments. getMRRHistory correctly uses lt nextMonth.
fix: Replace endDate with: const endDate = new Date(now.getFullYear(), now.getMonth() - i + 1, 1) and change lte: endDate to lt: endDate. This matches getMRRHistory's correct approach and is timezone-safe.
verdictReason: Verified at line 253: `new Date(now.getFullYear(), now.getMonth() - i + 1, 0)` creates day 0 (= last-day midnight), combined with `lte: endDate` creates a gap for late-day invoices in UTC-offset environments.

title: No composite DB indexes for analytics hot queries — full table scans on every load
detail: School analytics queries filter on (isActive, planType, createdAt) with no composite index — School has only PK. Invoice queries filter on (status, createdAt) for revenue trends, but Invoice only has @@index([schoolId]). As platform grows these become sequential scans on every analytics page hit. Needs schema change.
fix: Add to School model: @@index([isActive, planType, createdAt]). Add to Invoice model: @@index([status, createdAt]). These are additive and deployable via ALTER TABLE ... ADD INDEX without data migration. Needs schema change.
verdictReason: Verified: School model has no composite index; Invoice model only has @@index([schoolId]) at line 465. Analytics queries filter on (isActive, planType, createdAt) and (status, createdAt) with no supporting indexes.

title: calculateChurnRate and getAtRiskSchools exported but never rendered in any UI
detail: Both functions are fully implemented and guarded with requireOperator, but grep across all src/ components and pages finds zero call sites outside the test file. Operator analytics page (content.tsx) calls only calculateMRR, getMRRHistory, and getRevenueTrends. Churn rate and at-risk school list are invisible to the operator.
fix: Either wire these into the analytics content (add a Churn section and At-Risk Schools table to content.tsx) — the at-risk list is high-value for retention — or add a TODO comment and remove the exports to avoid dead-export confusion.
verdictReason: Grep confirmed zero call sites for calculateChurnRate/getAtRiskSchools in any UI component or page; only the test file imports them.

title: Month labels hardcoded to 'en-US' locale in server actions
detail: getMRRHistory (line 103) and getRevenueTrends (line 255) both call date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', year: 'numeric' }). Arabic operators will see English month abbreviations ('Jan', 'Feb') in the MRR chart axis and revenue trends table.
fix: Pass lang as a parameter to getMRRHistory(lang) and getRevenueTrends(lang), then call date.toLocaleDateString(lang, { month: 'short', year: 'numeric' }). content.tsx already has lang available from its Props interface.
verdictReason: Verified at lines 103 and 255: hardcoded 'en-US' locale string in both date formatting calls.

title: MRRChart client component — 3 user-visible strings hardcoded in English
detail: CardTitle 'MRR Trend' (line 40), CardDescription 'Monthly Recurring Revenue over the last 6 months' (line 42), and tooltip label 'Schools:' (line 98) are all hardcoded English. MRRChart receives only data and lang with no dictionary prop.
fix: Add a dictionary prop to MRRChartProps, pass it from content.tsx, and replace the three hardcoded strings with dictionary keys. Add mrrTrend, mrrTrendDescription, schools keys to the operator analytics dictionary namespace.
verdictReason: Verified: lines 40, 42, 98 contain hardcoded English strings; MRRChartProps interface has only `data` and `lang`, no dictionary prop.

title: MRRByPlan client component — all user-visible strings hardcoded in English
detail: Plan name labels 'Basic', 'Premium', 'Enterprise' (lines 37-39), CardTitle 'MRR by Plan' (line 47), CardDescription (line 49), 'Total MRR' label (line 54), and 'of total' tooltip suffix (line 97) are all hardcoded English. MRRByPlanProps has no dictionary prop.
fix: Add a dictionary prop to MRRByPlanProps, thread it from content.tsx (which has the full dictionary), and replace all static strings with dictionary keys.
verdictReason: Verified: all cited strings (lines 37-39, 47, 49, 54, 97) are hardcoded English literals; MRRByPlanProps interface has no dictionary field.

title: Invoice count pluralization uses English-only plural rules
detail: `{trend.invoices} invoice{trend.invoices !== 1 ? 's' : ''}` uses English plural morphology. Arabic has 6 grammatical plural forms and an -s suffix doesn't apply. This renders incorrectly for Arabic operators.
fix: Use Intl.PluralRules or a dictionary key with count interpolation. Add analytics.invoiceCount (singular/plural variants) to operator-en.json and operator-ar.json.
verdictReason: Verified at line 194: exact string `invoice{trend.invoices !== 1 ? 's' : ''}` — English-only plural, breaks for Arabic.

title: analytics/error.tsx: all user-visible strings hardcoded in English
detail: CardTitle 'Analytics Error' (line 36), CardDescription (line 38), button 'Refresh analytics' (line 44), and button 'Reload page' (line 49) are all hardcoded English. Error boundaries are client components and cannot use async getDictionary.
fix: Use a DictionaryContext/useDictionary hook (if provided by the layout) to access the dictionary client-side, or pre-bake a small static error-strings module per locale and import it directly. The layout already has lang and provides the dictionary context.
verdictReason: Verified hardcoded strings at lines 35, 38, 43, 48. Severity adjusted from P2 to P2 (unchanged), but confidence is medium because DictionaryContext availability in this layout needs verification before implementing the fix.

title: churnRate denominator includes already-inactive schools, understating churn rate
detail: schoolsAtStart counts all schools where createdAt < startDate with no isActive filter, including schools that were already inactive before the period began. The correct denominator is schools active at period start. A platform with 90 active + 10 pre-churned schools that loses 3 gets 3/100=3% instead of 3/90=3.33%; distortion grows over time.
fix: Change schoolsAtStart query to count where createdAt < startDate AND isActive: true (approximate; precise fix needs School.deactivatedAt schema field). Mark as needing schema change for the accurate version.
verdictReason: Verified at lines 162-168: schoolsAtStart query has no isActive filter, counting all schools ever created before the period including already-inactive ones.

title: No unstable_cache on any analytics function — full DB round-trip on every navigation
detail: All four analytics functions execute live DB queries on every page hit. MRR and revenue trends are slow-changing (at most daily) but computed fresh every render. tenants/queries.ts in the same block already uses unstable_cache for its queries. The analytics page has dynamic='force-dynamic' (correct for auth), but unstable_cache can still cache underneath.
fix: Wrap calculateMRR, getMRRHistory, and getRevenueTrends with unstable_cache({ revalidate: 3600 }) (1-hour TTL). Use the same pattern as src/components/saas-dashboard/tenants/queries.ts lines 38-46.
verdictReason: Verified: no unstable_cache or React cache() anywhere in analytics/actions.ts; tenants/queries.ts confirms the existing pattern is available in the same block.

title: getRevenueTrends fires 6 aggregate queries — consolidate to groupBy or raw SQL
detail: 6 db.invoice.aggregate calls run with Promise.all (parallel is good), but all can be collapsed to a single Prisma groupBy or one raw SQL query with DATE_TRUNC, reducing 6 network round-trips to 1.
fix: Use db.$queryRaw`SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) as month, SUM(amount_paid), COUNT(*) FROM "Invoice" WHERE status='paid' AND created_at >= ${sixMonthsAgo} GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1` — or add this to the unstable_cache wrapper (1-hour TTL) so the 6 parallel queries only fire hourly.
verdictReason: Verified: lines 264-286 run 6 separate db.invoice.aggregate calls in Promise.all; a single groupBy or raw query would eliminate 5 of 6 round-trips.

title: getReceiptStats fires 4 separate count queries — consolidate with groupBy
detail: getReceiptStats at lines 115-119 issues 4 parallel count queries: all, pending, approved, rejected. A single db.receipt.groupBy({ by: ['status'], _count: { id: true } }) returns all four counts in one round-trip.
fix: Replace the four count() calls with a single groupBy query and derive each count from the result map: `const counts = await db.receipt.groupBy({ by: ['status'], _count: { id: true } })`; then map/find each status bucket.
verdictReason: Confirmed: receipts/content.tsx lines 115-119 show four separate count() calls issuing four DB round-trips when one groupBy would suffice.

title: CSV export hardcodes Arabic locale for all date cells
detail: getInvoicesCSV at lines 232-235 calls formatDate(invoice.periodStart, 'ar'), formatDate(invoice.periodEnd, 'ar'), formatDate(invoice.createdAt, 'ar'), formatDate(invoice.updatedAt, 'ar') — all hardcoded to Arabic locale regardless of the operator's session language. The CSV audience is DEVELOPER operators who are likely using English.
fix: Default to 'en' for CSV export dates since this is an operator-level data export, not a user-facing document. Alternatively accept a lang parameter from the calling ExportButton and forward it to getInvoicesCSV.
verdictReason: Confirmed: billing/actions.ts lines 232-235 hardcode 'ar' as the locale for all four formatDate calls in the CSV export.

title: CSV injection via school.name / school.domain — formula prefix not sanitized
detail: getInvoicesCSV at lines 225-241 wraps each cell in double-quotes but does not sanitize leading formula characters. school.name and school.domain are user-controlled strings stored in DB. A school operator who sets name to =HYPERLINK("https://evil.com","click") would produce a formula-injected CSV that executes in Excel/Sheets on open.
fix: Add a sanitizeCsvCell helper: `(cell: string) => { const s = String(cell ?? ''); return /^[=+\-@|]/.test(s) ? `'${s}` : s; }` and apply it to each cell value before the `"${cell}"` wrapping in the CSV rows map.
verdictReason: Confirmed: billing/actions.ts lines 239-241 wrap cells with simple `"${cell}"` quoting. School name and domain at lines 227-228 are raw DB values with no formula-prefix sanitization.

title: receipts/upload.tsx toast messages and labels hardcoded English
detail: ErrorToast('Select a file') at line 59, SuccessToast('Receipt uploaded successfully') at line 75, ErrorToast(result.error?.message || 'Failed to upload receipt') at line 79, and ErrorToast at line 82 are all hardcoded English. Line 138 has 'Submitting...' hardcoded. Also, t.billing.viewInvoiceDetails at line 93 is semantically misused as an Invoice selector label.
fix: Add keys to dictionary.operator.billing.receipts: selectFile, uploadSuccess, uploadFailed, submitting. Use these dictionary keys for all toast calls and the submitting label. Fix the Invoice selector label to use a dedicated key (e.g. billing.receipts.selectInvoice) rather than reusing viewInvoiceDetails.
verdictReason: Confirmed: upload.tsx lines 59, 75, 79, 82 show ErrorToast/SuccessToast with hardcoded English strings. Line 138 has 'Submitting...' hardcoded. Line 93 misuses t.billing.viewInvoiceDetails as an invoice selector label.

title: invoiceUpdateStatus applies no state-machine guard — can void a paid invoice
detail: invoiceUpdateStatus accepts any target status for any invoice in any current state without fetching and checking the current state first. The UI always shows both Mark paid and Void buttons regardless of current status. canPayInvoice() and canVoidInvoice() helpers exist in util.ts but are never called server-side.
fix: In invoiceUpdateStatus, fetch the current invoice before updating: `const current = await db.invoice.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: validated.id }, select: { status: true } })`. Then apply guards: if target is 'paid' and !canPayInvoice(current.status) return error; if target is 'void' and !canVoidInvoice(current.status) return error. Also disable respective buttons in columns.tsx based on the row's current status.
verdictReason: Confirmed: billing/actions.ts lines 44-49 update invoice status without any prior state check. canPayInvoice/canVoidInvoice in util.ts are never called from billing/actions.ts (grep confirms zero usages outside util.ts and tests).

title: Duplicate where-clause logic across getInvoicesData, getInvoices, and getInvoicesCSV
detail: The identical Prisma invoice where-clause (status filter + OR search over stripeInvoiceId/school.name) is copy-pasted three times: getInvoicesData in content.tsx lines 39-63, getInvoices in actions.ts lines 87-110, and getInvoicesCSV in actions.ts lines 170-194. Any filter logic change must be applied in all three places.
fix: Extract a buildInvoiceWhere(filters: { status?: string; search?: string }) => Prisma.InvoiceWhereInput helper function into a billing/queries.ts file and import it from all three locations.
verdictReason: Confirmed: content.tsx lines 39-63 and actions.ts lines 87-110 and 170-194 contain byte-for-byte identical Prisma where clause construction for invoice filtering.

title: useBillingData hook calls /api/billing which does not exist
detail: useBillingData() at line 29 fetches /api/billing. No such API route exists in src/app (verified: find returns nothing). The hook is also never imported anywhere in the codebase — it is pure dead code that would cause a 404 if ever used.
fix: Delete src/components/saas-dashboard/billing/hooks/use-billing.ts entirely. The billing UI correctly uses server-side getInvoicesData and the getInvoices server action for client-side load-more.
verdictReason: Confirmed: no /api/billing route found in src/app. The hook file is the only file that references itself — grep for useBillingData across src returns only the hook file itself.

title: card.tsx / featured.tsx / all.tsx components are not rendered anywhere in live routes
detail: No page, route, or component in src/ imports from these three files. featured.tsx imports from card.tsx but neither is reachable from any app route. all.tsx is also unreachable. These three files add ~900 lines of dead client code with all-hardcoded English strings throughout.
fix: Confirm no references exist (grep shows none), then delete all three files. Also audit util.ts for exports only consumed by these files (formatDueStatus, getBillingPeriod, getInvoiceHealth, getDaysUntilDue, formatBillingPeriod, sortInvoices, isInvoiceOverdue) — if unused elsewhere, delete those too.
verdictReason: Confirmed no imports of card.tsx/featured.tsx/all.tsx from any src/app or src/components route. featured.tsx imports from card.tsx (internal chain), but both are leaf-dead. Confidence adjusted from high to medium only because featured.tsx internally imports card.tsx — double-verified by grep.

title: PLAN_PRICING is a hardcoded table instead of live Stripe price lookup
detail: PLAN_PRICING = { TRIAL: 0, BASIC: 99, PREMIUM: 299, ENTERPRISE: 999 } at lines 195-200 is used in analytics/actions.ts in four places to calculate MRR and plan breakdowns. If pricing changes in Stripe, this table silently goes stale. The TODO comment on line 194 acknowledges this.
fix: Either (a) store planPrice as an Int cents field on the School model (set via Stripe webhook on subscription create/update) and SUM directly from DB, or (b) cache live Stripe price lookups per deploy. Short-term: add a console.warn log if a planType is not found in PLAN_PRICING so stale data is surfaced in logs.
verdictReason: Confirmed: config.ts lines 195-200 define the hardcoded table. analytics/actions.ts imports PLAN_PRICING and uses it in four MRR/plan-breakdown calculations. This matches the acknowledged TODO comment.

title: approveContent has no idempotency guard — double-clicking Approve silently overwrites approvedAt
detail: approveContent unconditionally writes approvalStatus='APPROVED' and a fresh approvedAt=new Date() on every call regardless of current state. An operator who clicks Approve twice, or where another operator already approved, gets a silent success that updates the audit timestamp. For the Book case the sub-transaction has an existingSelection guard that prevents a duplicate BookSelection row, but the main approval write (book.update) still runs unconditionally.
fix: Add a status pre-check: const current = await db.<model>.findUnique({ where: { id }, select: { approvalStatus: true } }); if (current?.approvalStatus === 'APPROVED') return { success: false, error: 'already_approved' }. Alternatively, use updateMany({ where: { id, approvalStatus: 'PENDING' } }) and check the count.
verdictReason: Directly verified: no pre-check on current approvalStatus in any of the five content-type branches of approveContent.

title: approvedAt is set on REJECTION — semantic field naming bug leads to wrong audit trail
detail: The rejectionData object at line 328 includes approvedAt: new Date() when status is REJECTED. The same pattern is present in exam-approval-actions.ts: rejectExam (lines 170-174) and rejectExamTemplate (lines 333-337) both set approvedAt on rejection. Rejected items therefore appear to have an approvedAt timestamp, making audit reports and queries that filter by approvedAt produce misleading results.
fix: Needs schema change (add reviewedAt column to affected models) — mark as recommendation. Interim: null out approvedAt on rejection paths and rely on the existing reviewedAt field (already present in proposal-actions.ts) where available.
verdictReason: Directly verified: rejectionData in approval-actions.ts line 329 contains approvedAt: new Date(); rejectExam (line 173) and rejectExamTemplate (line 334) also set approvedAt on rejection.

title: Unbounded slug-dedup loop inside $transaction — N×findUnique round-trips, no iteration cap
detail: Each SUBJECT/CHAPTER/LESSON approval runs a while-loop inside the Prisma transaction that retries findUnique until it finds an unused slug. Each iteration is a serialized await inside the transaction, holding the connection for the duration. No max-iteration guard exists. For subjects with many slug collisions this can create a prolonged lock; adversarial submissions with identical names could spin many round-trips.
fix: Add a max-attempt guard (e.g. if (attempt > 50) throw new Error('slug_dedup_limit')). Better: append a short crypto-based suffix (crypto.randomUUID().slice(0,6)) after 3 failed attempts to bail out of the loop quickly.
verdictReason: Directly verified all three while loops in proposal-actions.ts at the cited lines; none has an iteration cap.

title: ProposalReviewContent is a pure CSR component — loading flash and waterfall on every visit
detail: ProposalReviewContent is marked 'use client' (line 1) and fetches proposals via getProposalsForReview() server action from a useEffect (lines 219-233). On every page load the user sees a loading spinner while the action round-trips. Unlike ApprovalContent which is a server component doing parallel DB queries with Promise.all at render time, proposals always incur render → hydrate → useEffect → server action → re-render waterfall.
fix: Convert ProposalReviewContent to a server component: move the getProposalsForReview call to the page.tsx (or a dedicated async server component wrapper), pass proposals as a prop, and make the filter-change interaction use URL search params + server-side filtering. The client filter select becomes a URL param, enabling direct linking too.
verdictReason: Directly verified: file begins with 'use client', loadProposals is called in useEffect at line 230, no server-side data prefetch.

title: Hardcoded English strings in approval-content.tsx — dictionary prop received but never used for UI chrome
detail: ApprovalContent declares dictionary in its Props interface at line 40 but only destructures lang at line 44. All five stat card titles and descriptions are hardcoded English: 'Total Pending', 'Items awaiting review', 'Questions', 'Pending questions', 'Materials & Assignments', 'Books', 'Pending books', 'Videos', 'Pending videos'. Per translation rules all user-visible text must use dictionary keys.
fix: Destructure dictionary from props and replace hardcoded strings with dictionary lookups. Add the keys to the operator catalog section of the en/ar dictionaries.
verdictReason: Directly verified: Props at line 40 includes dictionary, but the function signature at line 44 only destructures lang. All card strings are hardcoded English.

title: Hardcoded English strings throughout question-content.tsx and assignment-content.tsx card headers/page titles

Dictionary is in scope and passed to the table but not used for card chrome.

detail: create-question-dialog.tsx: toast.error('Question text is required') (83), toast.error('Points must be a positive number') (89), toast.success('Question created') (125), toast.error('Failed to create question') (130), 'Create Question' button (139, 267), 'Add a new question to the catalog question bank.' description (147-148). create-assignment-dialog.tsx: toast.error('Title is required') (69), toast.success('Assignment created') (108), toast.error('Failed to create assignment') (113), 'Create Assignment' (button and title). Time', 'Approval', 'Status', 'Usage'. The dictionary param in getQuestionColumns/getAssignmentColumns is only used for the ContentFlagsDialog, not column headers.

title: Hardcoded English in video-approve-dialog — labels, select options, toast, title, description
detail: The entire VideoApproveDialog UI is hardcoded English: toast.success('Video approved') (74), DialogTitle 'Approve video' (87), info-box labels 'Title'/'Instructor'/'Proposed audience'/'Proposed price', Label 'Visibility', all four SelectItem text values ('Public catalog (all schools)', 'Proposer's school only', 'Private', 'Paid (requires price)'), price/currency labels, 'Feature this video' checkbox label, 'Cancel'/'Approve' buttons. No dictionary prop.

verdictReason: Directly verified: no dictionary prop in the component interface, all visible strings are hardcoded English literals.

title: Subject detail page fires two sequential findUnique queries (slug then ID fallback)
detail: The page uses the ?? operator between two sequential await db.subject.findUnique calls (lines 26-42 and 43-58). Since the table links pass the cuid (not the slug), the slug lookup always fails and the id lookup always succeeds — two full round trips to Neon on every page load. Both queries include the identical chapters+lessons include tree.
fix: Replace with a single db.subject.findFirst({ where: { OR: [{ slug: subjectId }, { id: subjectId }] }, include: { chapters: { ... } } }) — one query covers both cases and eliminates the sequential penalty.
verdictReason: Code at lines 26-58 confirmed: two sequential await db.subject.findUnique calls connected by ??, with identical include trees duplicated in both calls.

title: book-content fires two redundant DB count queries whose values are computable from fetched rows
detail: The Promise.all (lines 29-54) fetches all books with select, then also fires db.book.count({ where: { approvalStatus: 'APPROVED' } }) and db.book.count({ where: { approvalStatus: 'PENDING' } }) — two extra round trips. Both values are computable from the already-fetched books array. material-content and assignment-content correctly compute these counts in JS without extra DB calls.
fix: Remove the two db.book.count() calls from the Promise.all. Compute approvedCount and pendingCount from the fetched books array: books.filter(b => b.approvalStatus === 'APPROVED').length and books.filter(b => b.approvalStatus === 'PENDING').length. Add approvalStatus to the select clause.
verdictReason: Lines 48-49 of book-content.tsx confirmed: db.book.count({ where: { approvalStatus: 'APPROVED' } }) and db.book.count({ where: { approvalStatus: 'PENDING' } }) run alongside a findMany that fetches all books — both counts are trivially derivable from the fetched data.

title: VideoManager always loads videos client-side via useEffect — loading flash and redundant refetch after add
detail: VideoManager fetches videos with a getVideos server action call inside useEffect on every mount (lines 57-74), so users always see a loading spinner on dialog open. After successfully adding a video, handleAddVideo (line 107) calls getVideos again as a full refetch instead of optimistically appending the created video to local state.
fix: Pass initial videos as a prop from the parent server component (the parent CatalogDetail already receives the full subject tree). For the post-create refresh, append the created video's data returned by createVideo to local state instead of re-fetching. This eliminates the spinner on open and the redundant round trip after creation.
verdictReason: useEffect loading at lines 57-74 and getVideos refetch at line 107 both confirmed. The dialog always shows a spinner on open; post-add triggers a full refetch rather than optimistic update.

detail: Every card title ("Subjects", "Questions", "Materials", "Assignments", "Videos", "Total Content"), table header ("Subject", "Department", "Chapters", "Lessons", "Schools", "Rating"), section title ("Top Subjects by Usage", "Top Lessons by Usage"), and empty-state message ("No subjects yet.", "No lessons yet.") is hardcoded English. The component signature at line 39 destructures only { lang }, discarding the dictionary prop declared in the Props interface at line 34.

verdictReason: analytics-content.tsx line 39 destructures only { lang } despite Props (line 34) declaring dictionary. All card titles, table headers, and empty states at the cited lines are hardcoded English strings.
