title: Hardcoded English strings in image-upload.tsx toasts and dropzone labels
detail: toast.success("Thumbnail uploaded") line 57, toast.success("Thumbnail removed") line 72, dropzone text "Drop image here" / "Replace thumbnail" / "Upload thumbnail" lines 141-145, and button text "Remove CDN thumbnail" line 161 are all hardcoded English. The component has no dictionary prop.
fix: Add a dictionary prop to CatalogImageUpload, thread it from the parent server component, and replace all string literals with dictionary key lookups.
verdictReason: All five hardcoded strings confirmed at the cited lines. CatalogImageUpload has no dictionary prop in its interface (lines 18-23).

title: create-subject-dialog: serial N+M server round trips for chapter+lesson batch creation
detail: After creating a subject, the dialog creates chapters and lessons in a nested for loop with sequential awaits (lines 192-215): for each chapter, await createChapter, then for each lesson, await createLesson. Each createChapter runs 3 DB queries internally and createLesson runs 5. For a subject with 10 chapters of 5 lessons each, this is 10 + 50 = 60 sequential server action calls, each with multiple DB round trips.
fix: Add a new server action createSubjectWithStructure that accepts the full subject + chapters + lessons tree and creates everything in a single $transaction. At minimum, parallelize per-chapter lesson creation with Promise.all (chapters must be created serially to get their IDs, but lessons within a chapter are independent).
verdictReason: Nested for loops with sequential await at lines 192-215 confirmed. createChapter at line 201 is awaited inside an outer chapter loop, and createLesson at line 214 is awaited inside an inner lesson loop — no parallelization at any level.

title: Duplicate CatalogImageUpload rendered twice for the same subject entity
detail: The subject detail page renders CatalogImageUpload for entityType='subject' with the same entityId and currentThumbnailKey twice — once as a standalone "Banner Image Upload" section (lines 770-777) and again inside a "Thumbnail" Card (lines 810-826). Both components maintain independent local useState for the thumbnail key, so uploading via one does not update the other. The operator sees two upload widgets that both write to the same S3 key.
fix: Remove the standalone Banner Image Upload block (lines 770-777). The Thumbnail card (lines 810-826) is the canonical location for subject image management.
verdictReason: Lines 770-777 and 810-826 both render CatalogImageUpload with entityType='subject', entityId={subject.id}, currentThumbnailKey={subject.thumbnail} — identical props, two separate component instances with independent state.

title: Zod validation schemas contain hardcoded English error messages surfaced to users
detail: catalogSubjectSchema, catalogChapterSchema, and catalogLessonSchema use hardcoded English strings ("Name is required", "Slug is required", "Department is required") as Zod refinement messages. book-validation.ts lines 7-12 has "Title is required", "Slug is required", "Author is required", "Genre is required". question-validation.ts line 11 has "Question text is required". These strings propagate to the client via error.message in the actions' catch blocks and get toasted verbatim.
fix: Server actions should return error codes (e.g., errorCode: 'INVALID_INPUT') rather than Zod's English messages. Catch ZodError specifically and return { success: false, errorCode: 'INVALID_INPUT', fields: error.flatten().fieldErrors } so clients can translate field errors via the dictionary.
verdictReason: Hardcoded English strings confirmed in validation.ts (lines 7, 8, 11, 29, 30, 43, 44), book-validation.ts (lines 7-10, 12), and question-validation.ts (line 11). The pattern violates the project translation rules requiring error codes not English strings.

title: Math.random() used for slug generation on non-ASCII proposal names
detail: generateSlug falls back to Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10) for non-ASCII (Arabic) names, producing an 8-character base-36 string. Math.random() is not a CSPRNG and the resulting slug loses all semantic meaning (e.g., 'subject-a3b7z9').
fix: Use crypto.randomBytes(6).toString('hex') (12 hex chars, cryptographically random) or nanoid() for the fallback suffix. For Arabic names, consider using the existing transliterate() utility to produce a meaningful romanized slug before falling back to random.
verdictReason: Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10) confirmed at line 99 in proposal-actions.ts. While collision risk is low in practice, the pattern is a correctness smell in a server action and produces non-semantic slugs for Arabic content.

title: Hardcoded English strings in create-subject-dialog and create-material-dialog
detail: toast.error("Name is required") line 155, toast.error("Department is required") line 158 (repeated at 231-232), toast.error("Failed to create subject") line 183, toast.success("Subject created") line 218, toast.error("Failed to create subject") line 223, and ModalFooter label strings at lines 611-617 ("Cancel", "Back", "Next", "Create", "Creating...") are all hardcoded. create-material-dialog.tsx lines 64-65 ("Title is required"), 95 ("Material created"), 100 ("Failed to create material") are also hardcoded.

verdictReason: create-subject-dialog.tsx lines 155, 158, 183, 218, 223, 231 and ModalFooter labels at 611-617 all confirmed as hardcoded English. create-material-dialog.tsx lines 64, 95, 100 also confirmed.

title: ResourceUsageSection role=DEVELOPER mixes two real rows with two permanently mocked rows

Rows 3-4 are always { name: 'Database Size', used: 120, limit: 500, unit: 'GB' } and { name: 'System Uptime', used: 99.9, limit: 100, unit: '%' } with explicit '// Mocked' comments. Displayed in the same DetailedUsageTable with no visual distinction.

verdictReason: getDeveloperResourceUsage() at actions.ts line 3686-3687 confirmed returning hardcoded Database Size=120 GB and System Uptime=99.9% with // Mocked comments alongside real db counts.

title: Weather widget always returns null for DEVELOPER — getTenantContext() has no schoolId
detail: content.tsx calls getWeatherData('metric', lang) in the Promise.all. getWeatherData calls getTenantContext() and returns null if schoolId is missing (weather-actions.ts lines 77-78). DEVELOPER session always has schoolId=null. The call wastes a round-trip on every page load and always produces null.

verdictReason: getWeatherData confirmed in Promise.all at content.tsx line 26; weather-actions.ts lines 77-78 confirmed returning null when schoolId is missing; DEVELOPER role confirmed to have null schoolId.

title: bar-graph-skeleton.tsx uses Math.random() in JSX causing hydration mismatch
detail: Skeleton style prop: { height: `${Math.max(20, Math.random() * 100)}%` }. Math.random() produces different values on server and client, causing React hydration mismatch warning. Component is currently dead code but would become a runtime error if wired to a Suspense boundary.
fix: Replace Math.random() with a fixed deterministic array of heights (e.g. [80,45,65,90,30,75,55,85,40,70,60,50]) indexed by i.
verdictReason: Math.random() in style prop confirmed at bar-graph-skeleton.tsx line 35; confirmed no imports of BarGraphSkeleton outside its own definition file.

detail:

Both accept a dictionary prop that is unused for these strings.
fix: Replace each string with a corresponding dictionary key from dictionary?.operator?.products. If the product concept is being dropped, delete both files as part of the dead-code sweep.
verdictReason: All cited strings verified in both files; dictionary prop is accepted but not used for any of them.

title: product-form.tsx has hardcoded Zod validation messages and field labels
detail: Confirmed: Zod schema uses bare English strings — 'Image is required.' (line 44), 'Product name must be at least 2 characters.' (line 46), 'Description must be at least 10 characters.' (line 51). FormLabels hardcoded: 'Category' (line 141), 'Price' (line 183), 'Description' (line 199). Button text 'Add Product' (line 216) hardcoded despite p = dictionary?.operator?.products being available and used for some other fields.
fix: Wrap Zod schema in a factory function accepting ValidationHelper for messages. Replace hardcoded labels with p?.category, p?.price, p?.description, p?.addProduct dictionary keys.
verdictReason: All seven cited locations verified; inconsistent — some fields use p?.productName pattern correctly while Category/Price/Description/Add Product are hardcoded.

title: isMounted useEffect has stale dependency — re-fires unnecessarily after mount
detail: Confirmed: useEffect(() => { setIsMounted(true) }, [isMounted]) at lines 73-75. Including isMounted in the dependency array causes the effect to re-run after the first render sets isMounted to true (isMounted changed → effect fires again → calls setIsMounted(true) again → React sees state update → extra render). The correct pattern is an empty dependency array since the intent is to run once on mount.
fix: Change line 75 to: }, []) — empty dependency array.
verdictReason: Line 75 confirmed as }, [isMounted]); stale dependency causes at minimum one extra render cycle on mount.

title: card.tsx, featured.tsx, all.tsx, util.ts, hooks/use-domains.ts, types.ts, create-request.tsx, and validation.ts are entirely dead code
lines: card.tsx:1, featured.tsx:1, all.tsx:1, util.ts:1, hooks/use-domains.ts:1, types.ts:1, create-request.tsx:1, validation.ts:1
detail: None of these files is imported from any live production file. They form a self-referencing island: all.tsx→card.tsx→util.ts→config.ts→types.ts, featured.tsx→card.tsx/util.ts, hooks/use-domains.ts→validation.ts (which calls a non-existent /api/domains REST route). create-request.tsx exports CreateDomainRequest which is imported nowhere. grep across the entire src/ tree confirms zero live imports.
fix: Delete card.tsx, featured.tsx, all.tsx, util.ts, hooks/use-domains.ts, types.ts, create-request.tsx, validation.ts. Keep config.ts only if any live file imports from it; otherwise delete. Consolidate DomainRow type to columns.tsx exclusively.
verdictReason: Grep across entire src/ tree found zero imports of these files from any live file. They cross-reference each other but are unreachable from content.tsx, the page, or the app.

title: DomainRow.notes mapped in content.tsx but absent from both DomainRow type definitions — data is silently discarded
detail: content.tsx maps request.notes (line 86) into DomainRow objects, but neither DomainRow in columns.tsx (lines 10-16) nor DomainRow in table.tsx (lines 14-20) declares a notes field. TypeScript's structural typing silently accepts the extra property. The notes data is fetched from DB, mapped, passed through, but never surfaced to the operator in the table or any tooltip.
fix: Delete the duplicate DomainRow in table.tsx. Add notes?: string | null to the canonical DomainRow in columns.tsx. Add a Notes column or surface notes in a tooltip/detail popover in the columns definition.
verdictReason: Verified: columns.tsx DomainRow (lines 10-16) has no notes; table.tsx DomainRow (lines 14-20) has no notes; content.tsx line 86 maps notes; the field is fetched but never rendered.

title: columns.tsx: all column headers, status labels, and filter placeholders are hardcoded English
detail: Every visible string in domainColumns is hardcoded English: headers 'School', 'Domain', 'Status', 'Created'; meta labels and placeholders 'Search school', 'Search domain'; status badge options 'Pending', 'Approved', 'Rejected', 'Verified'; and the inline capitalizer (v.charAt(0).toUpperCase()+v.slice(1)) which silently fails for Arabic. The component receives no dictionary prop. The operator-en.json dictionary has 'domains' keys available.
fix: Convert domainColumns to a factory function getDomainColumns(t: Dictionary['operator']['domains']) and call it from content.tsx with the resolved dictionary. Pass translated strings for each header, label, placeholder, and status option.
verdictReason: Verified: columns.tsx has no Props interface or dictionary param; all strings on lines 22-76 are hardcoded English; operator-en.json already has domain column translation keys.

title: error.tsx: card title, description, and button label are hardcoded English; error.message leaks internal detail
detail: The error boundary renders 'Domain Management Error', 'Failed to load domain requests. Please try again.', and 'Retry loading domains' as hardcoded JSX strings. Additionally, error.message is rendered directly at line 44 which may expose Prisma/DB error details to the operator.
fix: Use a client-side dictionary via DictionaryProvider context or useDictionary() hook (error.tsx cannot be async). Replace hardcoded strings with dictionary keys. Guard error.message behind process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' or strip it entirely from the public display.
verdictReason: Verified: error.tsx lines 36, 38-39, 49 have hardcoded English strings; line 44 renders {error.message} unconditionally.

title: generateVerificationToken in util.ts is insecure (predictable base64) and completely unused dead code
detail: The function encodes schoolId+domain+Date.now() as base64 truncated to 32 chars — trivially reversible and predictable. The comment acknowledges 'use crypto in production'. The function is never imported by any live file (util.ts itself is dead code).
fix: Delete the function from util.ts (or delete util.ts entirely as it is dead). If domain ownership verification is ever needed, use crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex') stored in a DomainRequest.verificationToken column.
verdictReason: Verified: util.ts line 161-170 contains the insecure function; no live file imports from saas-dashboard/domains/util.ts — only dead files card.tsx, all.tsx, and featured.tsx import it.

title: config.ts REQUIRED_DNS_RECORDS has placeholder 'schoolapp.com' values; tenants/card.tsx renders them live
detail: REQUIRED_DNS_RECORDS exports CNAME value 'cname.schoolapp.com' and TXT value 'schoolapp-verification=' — scaffold placeholders, not real platform endpoints. config.ts is dead code so these are not rendered from domains. However tenants/card.tsx lines 64 and 230 hardcode 'schoolapp.com' directly in JSX ('tenant.domain.schoolapp.com'), and tenants/util.ts line 28 sets baseUrl='schoolapp.com'. These ARE rendered live and show fictional URLs to the operator.
fix: Source DNS targets from environment variables already present (VERCEL_CNAME_TARGET is read in domainVerify). In tenants/card.tsx and tenants/util.ts replace 'schoolapp.com' with process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PLATFORM_DOMAIN or a central config constant. Delete REQUIRED_DNS_RECORDS from config.ts or rebuild using env vars.
verdictReason: config.ts REQUIRED_DNS_RECORDS is dead code so no rendered impact there. The real fabricated-data bug is in tenants/card.tsx (lines 64, 230) and tenants/util.ts (line 28) which hardcode 'schoolapp.com' and ARE rendered live. Severity stays P2.

title: domainApprove and domainReject bypass the FSM — verified domains can be re-rejected or re-approved
detail: Both actions call db.domainRequest.update() directly without checking current status. ALLOWED_STATUS_TRANSITIONS is defined in config.ts and correctly specifies that only pending→approved and pending→rejected are valid, but config.ts is never imported by actions.ts. A verified domain can be re-rejected; a rejected domain can be approved — corrupting domain lifecycle state.
fix: Before each update, fetch the current record and verify canTransitionStatus(current.status, newStatus). Return errorCode: 'INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION' if disallowed. Alternatively add a where: { status: 'pending' } clause so a 0-row update is detectable as an error.
verdictReason: Verified: actions.ts imports nothing from config.ts; domainApprove line 70 and domainReject line 111 do unconditional updates with no status pre-check.

title: getDomainRequestsData in content.tsx duplicates getDomains action logic verbatim — two query paths to maintain
detail: getDomainRequestsData (content.tsx lines 31-95) is structurally identical to the exported getDomains server action (actions.ts lines 262-344): same where-clause building, same include, same row mapping. Any future filter change must be applied in both places or silently diverges.
fix: Delete getDomainRequestsData from content.tsx. Call getDomains(searchParams) directly and unwrap the ActionResult. Adapt the call signature to match getDomains's { page, perPage, status, search } input.
verdictReason: Verified: both functions perform identical where-clause construction, identical findMany with school include, identical row mapping. The only difference is parameter naming (page/limit vs page/perPage).

title: Deprecated auditColumns (no dictionary) used in production — column headers always render hardcoded English
detail: content.tsx imports the deprecated auditColumns constant (columns.tsx line 99: export const auditColumns = getAuditColumns() with no dictionary) and passes it to AuditLogTable at line 103. The getAuditColumns(dictionary) overload exists and is the intended API, but is not used. All column headers (Time, User, IP, School, Action, Level, Request ID, Reason) always show in English regardless of locale.
fix: In content.tsx, replace import { auditColumns, ... } with import { getAuditColumns, ... }. Pass const columns = getAuditColumns(dictionary) where dictionary is already available in the component. Thread columns through to AuditLogTable.
verdictReason: content.tsx line 9 imports the deprecated auditColumns; columns.tsx line 99 exports it with no dictionary arg; getAuditColumns(dictionary) overload exists at line 21.

title: provider.ts missing import 'server-only' — Prisma db import and LOG_API_TOKEN reachable from client bundle if imported by a client component
detail: provider.ts imports db (Prisma client) and reads process.env.LOG_API_TOKEN but has no import 'server-only' guard. If a client component ever imports it, Next.js will bundle it client-side, potentially leaking the API token. conference/queries.ts correctly has import 'server-only' at line 9. content.tsx also lacks this guard. Currently provider.ts is dead code, but the pattern is a defect.
fix: Add import 'server-only' as the first import in both provider.ts and content.tsx. Zero-cost defensive measure.
verdictReason: provider.ts has no server-only guard; reads LOG_API_TOKEN and imports db; conference/queries.ts sets the correct precedent.

title: NEXT_PUBLIC_LOG_PROVIDER env var inlines log backend choice into the client JS bundle
detail: NEXT_PUBLIC_LOG_PROVIDER is a NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefixed variable; Next.js statically inlines its value into the client JS bundle at build time. The variable reveals which logging backend is in use (db vs http), which is internal infrastructure information. Provider.ts is server-only code that should never need a NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix.
fix: Rename to LOG_PROVIDER (no NEXT_PUBLIC_) in .env and provider.ts. Since provider.ts is only ever called from server-side code, process.env.LOG_PROVIDER works fine without the prefix.
verdictReason: provider.ts line 37 uses NEXT_PUBLIC_LOG_PROVIDER; this prefix causes Next.js to inline the value into the client bundle regardless of import context.

title: Silent error swallowing in content.tsx hides real DB failures — operator sees empty table indistinguishable from no data
detail: The catch block at line 95 swallows all errors silently and renders rows = [] with the same empty-state UI as legitimately having no audit logs. A broken DB query, schema mismatch, or connection failure looks identical to an empty audit table. The error.tsx boundary at src/app/[lang]/(saas-dashboard)/observability/error.tsx exists and is wired but is never reached because the error is caught here.
fix: Remove the broad catch or at minimum log server-side and re-throw: catch (err) { console.error('[observability] audit query failed:', err); throw err; }. The error boundary will display a user-facing message without leaking stack traces.
verdictReason: content.tsx lines 95-98: bare catch { rows = [] } with no logging or rethrow; error.tsx boundary at the route level is confirmed but bypassed.

title: Hardcoded English strings in error.tsx — 'Observability Error', error description, 'Refresh logs', 'Reload page'
detail: The error boundary component (which IS live and reachable) has fully hardcoded English CardTitle, CardDescription, and both Button labels. Error boundaries are client components so cannot call getDictionary() directly.
fix: Call useDictionaryContext() (already exported from src/components/internationalization/dictionary-context.tsx) in the error component — the saas-dashboard layout wraps with DictionaryProvider (layout.tsx line 64). Replace hardcoded strings with dictionary keys.
verdictReason: error.tsx lines 35, 38-39, 45, 52 confirmed hardcoded English; DictionaryProvider wraps the layout at src/app/[lang]/(saas-dashboard)/layout.tsx line 64; useDictionaryContext exported.

title: Email search via performer relation JOIN forces full-scan — no action-only index for cross-tenant search
detail: When search is set, the WHERE uses OR [performer.email CONTAINS ..., action CONTAINS ...]. This triggers a relation JOIN through users with ILIKE on email — the composite unique on [email, schoolId] cannot cover a bare ILIKE scan. Combined with action CONTAINS (another ILIKE), neither the [schoolId, action] nor the [createdAt] index is usable for this cross-tenant query, resulting in sequential scan on audit_logs joined with users. There is no action-only index.
fix: Add @@index([action]) to AuditLog for global action search. For email search, denormalize by storing performerEmail String? directly on AuditLog at write time (needs schema: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS), which avoids the JOIN and allows an @@index([performerEmail]).
verdictReason: content.tsx lines 40-60 confirmed; AuditLog indexes are [schoolId], [schoolId,userId], [schoolId,entityType,entityId], [schoolId,action], [createdAt] — no action-only or email index.

title: exportLogsToCSV — cell values not escaped for CSV formula injection or embedded double-quotes
detail: Each cell is wrapped in double-quotes (line 320: `"${cell}"`) but the cell content is not sanitized. A log action or reason string starting with =, +, -, @ would be interpreted as a formula by Excel/LibreOffice. Embedded double-quotes in cell values are not escaped (should be doubled). Audit log action names and reason fields are user-controlled and could be crafted by a malicious tenant admin.
fix: Escape cell values: const escapeCell = (s: string) => { const str = String(s ?? ''); const safe = /^[=+\-@\t\r]/.test(str) ? `'${str}` : str; return `"${safe.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`; }. Use escapeCell in place of the bare template literal.
verdictReason: util.ts line 320: row.map((cell) => `"${cell}"`).join(',') — no formula prefix stripping, no double-quote escaping.

title: conference/content.tsx: toLocaleString() called server-side reflects server timezone, not user's timezone
detail: e.occurredAt.toLocaleString(lang === 'ar' ? 'ar' : 'en') is called in an async RSC (no 'use client' directive). The output reflects the server's timezone (UTC on Vercel) — operators in other timezones will see incorrect local times for conference events. The <time dateTime={e.occurredAt.toISOString()}> wrapper is present which is good, but the human-readable text is still wrong.
fix: Remove the toLocaleString() inner text and instead rely on CSS/JS to format the <time> element client-side. A small 'use client' wrapper around the timestamp list or suppressHydrationWarning on the <time> element with client-side Intl.DateTimeFormat formatting would fix this.
verdictReason: conference/content.tsx has no 'use client' directive; line 109 calls toLocaleString() inside an async server component function; server is UTC on Vercel.

title: Duplicate UnifiedLog type definitions with conflicting userId nullability — types.ts non-nullable vs provider.ts nullable
detail: types.ts exports interface UnifiedLog { userId: string } (non-nullable, line 23), while provider.ts exports type UnifiedLog = { userId: string | null } (nullable, line 23). The Prisma AuditLog model has userId String? (nullable, for system actors like cron/workers). all.tsx, featured.tsx, card.tsx, util.ts import from types.ts; provider.ts uses its own definition. The types.ts definition is incorrect relative to the Prisma model.
fix: Fix types.ts UnifiedLog.userId to string | null. Delete the duplicate UnifiedLog export from provider.ts and have it import from types.ts instead. Update any downstream code that assumed userId is always non-null.
verdictReason: types.ts line 23: userId: string; provider.ts line 23: userId: string | null; AuditLog Prisma model: userId String? (nullable).

title: Correctness: orphanedUsers query has redundant NOT clause (silently under-counts)
detail: { schoolId: null, role: 'USER', NOT: { role: 'DEVELOPER' } } — the NOT clause is redundant since role='USER' already excludes DEVELOPER. Whether to also count schoolId=null users with ADMIN/TEACHER/STAFF/etc roles is a product question; the subtitle says 'No school, role USER' suggesting USER-only is intentional. The redundant NOT is confirmed code smell.
fix: Remove the redundant NOT clause: { schoolId: null, role: 'USER' }. If broader orphan coverage is desired later, drop the role filter and add NOT:{ role:'DEVELOPER' } as the only exclusion.
verdictReason: Redundant NOT clause is confirmed real. However the subtitle text 'No school, role USER' confirms USER-only is intentional product semantics, so the 'under-counts orphans' framing is overstated — this is a code-clarity issue, not a metric bug. Severity adjusted to P3.

title: Perf: missing DB index on Lead.nextFollowUpAt causes full table scan for overdue/cadence filters
detail: The dueBefore filter queries nextFollowUpAt: { lte: ..., not: null }. Lead has indexes on schoolId, status, source, leadType, assignedToId, score — but not on nextFollowUpAt. At scale this will full-scan the leads table. LeadActivity also has no index on createdAt, yet the weekly cadence groupBy filters by createdAt: { gte: weekStart } on every analytics page load.
fix: Add @@index([schoolId, nextFollowUpAt]) to Lead and @@index([schoolId, createdAt]) to LeadActivity in sales.prisma. Apply via ALTER TABLE on Neon (no migration reset needed).
verdictReason: Prisma schema confirmed: no nextFollowUpAt index on Lead (lines 106-111) and no createdAt index on LeadActivity (lines 133-135).

title: i18n: entire users block has hardcoded English strings
detail: columns.tsx: header titles (Email, Username, Role, School, Status, Created), filter option labels (Developer, Admin, etc.), cell text (Suspended, Active, No school), dropdown labels (Actions, Detach from school, Suspend, Unsuspend, Delete User), prompt() strings. table.tsx: searchPlaceholder 'Search users...', empty-state text, grid labels No email/No school/Loading.../Load More. delete-dialog.tsx: title 'Delete User', confirm label, button text 'Deleting...'. analysis.tsx: all four card titles and subtitles. None use dictionary keys.
fix: Accept a dictionary prop in all client components (passed from the page server component). Extract all strings to dictionary.operator.users.* keys in both en/ar JSON files. Pass dictionary={dictionary} from UsersContent/UsersAnalysis.
verdictReason: All cited files confirmed to have hardcoded English strings with no dictionary backing.

title: i18n: hardcoded English strings in sales analytics-content (cadence card labels, section headings)
detail: weeklyCadence, weeklySubtitle, tierFunnel, tierFunnelSubtitle, mena10, mena10Subtitle, metricNewWarm, metricOutreach, metricDiscovery, metricProposals, metricPilots, targetWeek, targetMonth are all hardcoded English fallback strings. None exist as keys in dictionaries/en/sales.json (which does have analytics.totalLeads, analytics.statusDistribution, etc. but none of the cadence/funnel keys). The dictionary prop is typed as optional so missing dictionary renders all-English.
fix: Add missing keys (analytics.weeklyCadence, analytics.tierFunnel, analytics.mena10, analytics.metricNewWarm, etc.) to both en/ar sales.json. Mark dictionary prop as required. Remove hardcoded English fallbacks.
verdictReason: Confirmed code at lines 119-139 and verified sales.json dictionary — none of the cadence/funnel keys exist.

title: Security: UsersAnalysis and UsersContent server components have no direct auth check
detail: getUserStats() runs 4 db.user.count() queries and UsersContent runs db.user.findMany/count with no requireOperator() at the component level. They rely entirely on the layout.tsx gate (which checks session.user?.role !== 'DEVELOPER'). This differs from analytics-content.tsx which correctly calls await requireOperator() at line 51 as defense-in-depth.
fix: Add await requireOperator() at the top of getUserStats() (in analysis.tsx) and getUsers() (in content.tsx) as defense-in-depth, consistent with the pattern used in analytics-content.tsx.
verdictReason: Confirmed: neither analysis.tsx nor content.tsx calls requireOperator(); layout.tsx uses manual role check (not requireOperator()), creating an inconsistent defense pattern.

title: Rendering: browser prompt() used for suspend/detach reason — blocks thread and silently ignores cancellation
detail: prompt() is used to collect suspension/detach reason. The || '' coercion means cancellation fires the action with an empty reason rather than aborting it. prompt() is also unstyled, blocks the browser thread, and may be suppressed by CSP or popup-blocking policies.
fix: Replace with a small AlertDialog + Textarea (following the existing DeleteUserDialog pattern). Cancellation should abort the action entirely by returning early from the handler.
verdictReason: Confirmed at lines 179-181 and 197: prompt() with || '' coercion; cancellation (null return) becomes empty string and the action still fires.

title: Correctness: startOfWeek anchors on Sunday while UI label says Monday resets counters
detail: startOfWeek uses d.getDay() where 0=Sunday, making Sunday the week boundary. But weeklySubtitle (line 127) says 'Monday resets the counters' and table.tsx line 59 notes 'Sunday-week: bump to next Saturday... so a Monday plan still shows Friday's calls'. This means work done Monday–Saturday of the prior week is not counted on Monday morning when the operator reviews cadence.
fix: Use ISO week start (Monday): const day = d.getDay(); d.setDate(d.getDate() - ((day + 6) % 7)); d.setHours(0,0,0,0); return d;
verdictReason: Confirmed: line 40-41 uses getDay()=0 for Sunday and subtracts it; weeklySubtitle at line 127 contradicts this with 'Monday resets the counters'.

title: i18n: import-content.tsx has hardcoded English strings not backed by dictionary
detail: csvImport ('CSV Upload'), csvHelp ('Headers: name (required), email, phone...'), and chooseFile ('Choose CSV file') at lines 88-96 are hardcoded and absent from the import dictionary keys. Line 254: `{parsed.length} {parsed.length === 1 ? 'lead' : 'leads'}` and line 291: `+{parsed.length - 50} more` are also hardcoded English.
fix: Add dictionary.sales.import.csvImport, csvHelp, chooseFile keys in both en/ar sales.json. Move count string to a dictionary template key (e.g., import.leadsDetected with {count} interpolation).
verdictReason: Confirmed: lines 88-96 have hardcoded strings not in the t object (which does use i?.csvImport - but the key doesn't exist in the sales.json import section). Lines 254 and 291 have additional hardcoded English.

title: Churn rate metric uses updatedAt as proxy for deactivation, producing false positives
detail: calculateChurnRate counts schools where isActive:false AND updatedAt >= startDate. Any admin edit or migration touching an inactive school bumps updatedAt and re-classifies it as newly churned. A school deactivated 6 months ago but touched today counts as current-period churn. The code has a TODO comment acknowledging this at line 152. Needs School.deactivatedAt (schema change).
fix: Short-term: show the metric with a '(approximate)' disclaimer in the UI. Long-term: add School.deactivatedAt DateTime? field (schema change, effort L) and filter on that instead of updatedAt.
verdictReason: Confirmed at lines 173-176: where { isActive: false, updatedAt: { gte: startDate } }. The code's own TODO at line 152 acknowledges the proxy is inaccurate.

title: nav-user dropdown: all visible labels are hardcoded English with no dictionary prop
detail: 'Upgrade to Pro' (line 84), 'Account' (line 91), 'Billing' (line 94), 'Notifications' (line 98), 'Log out' (line 103) are hardcoded. The component accepts only a user prop with no dictionary prop.
fix: Add dictionary?: Record<string, string> prop, pass from the sidebar host, and use dictionary?.upgradetoPro ?? 'Upgrade to Pro' etc. Add keys to operator dictionaries en/ar.
verdictReason: Confirmed: all five strings are hardcoded at the cited lines; component signature has no dictionary prop.

title: nav-main 'Platform' group label and nav-projects 'Projects' + action labels are hardcoded English
detail: NavMain renders <SidebarGroupLabel>Platform</SidebarGroupLabel> (line 41) with no dictionary prop. NavProjects (nav-projects.tsx) renders 'Projects' (line 38) and 'View Project', 'Share Project', 'Delete Project', 'More' in dropdown items. NavProjects is not imported in any production route (no usages found outside its own file), but NavMain is used in the production sidebar.
fix: Add dictionary? prop to NavMain; pass translated groupLabel from sidebar host. NavProjects can be deleted (it is dead code) or similarly updated if wired later.
verdictReason: Confirmed: 'Platform' hardcoded at nav-main.tsx:41; nav-projects.tsx has hardcoded 'Projects'/'View Project' etc.; NavMain is used in production sidebar; NavProjects has zero imports outside its own file.

title: search-input placeholder 'Search...' hardcoded English
detail: 'Search...' is hardcoded (line 20). Component accepts no dictionary prop. Used as global command-palette trigger in the platform header.
fix: Add dictionary?: any prop, pass from header, use dictionary?.common?.search ?? 'Search...'.
verdictReason: Confirmed at line 20: hardcoded 'Search...' string, no dictionary prop in component signature.

title: saas-client.tsx section headings 'Quick Look', 'Quick Actions', 'Recent Sales' hardcoded despite dictionary prop being available
detail: SaasDashboardClient receives dictionary: Dictionary as prop but passes hardcoded English strings to SectionHeading: 'Quick Look' (line 62), 'Quick Actions' (line 68), 'Recent Sales' (line 86).
fix: Use dictionary?.operator?.dashboard?.quickLook ?? 'Quick Look' etc. and add keys to the operator dictionary slice.
verdictReason: Confirmed at lines 62, 68, 86: hardcoded strings passed to SectionHeading; dictionary prop is received (line 27) but not used for these headings.

title: file-uploader.tsx: all toast and drop-zone messages are hardcoded English
detail: toast.error('Cannot upload more than 1 file') (146), toast.error('Cannot upload more than ${maxFiles} files') (151), toast.error('File ${file.name} was rejected') (167), toast.promise loading/success/error strings (180-185), 'Drop the files here' (247), 'Drag n drop files here' (260) are all hardcoded. Component accepts no dictionary prop and is actively used by billing/receipts.

title: compliance/shared-groups-table.tsx: toast.success and button labels hardcoded English
detail: toast.success('Group created') at line 91, button text '{pending ? "Saving…" : "Create"}' at line 167, and <Button>Add credential group</Button> at line 107 are hardcoded. Component receives dict: ComplianceDict but these strings are not in the dict type.
fix: Add groupCreated, saving, create, addGroup keys to ComplianceDict type and both locale files, then reference: toast.success(dict.groupCreated), {pending ? dict.saving : dict.create}, <Button>{dict.addGroup}</Button>.

title: billing/receipts/upload.tsx: ErrorToast and SuccessToast messages are hardcoded English
detail: ErrorToast('Select a file') (line 59), SuccessToast('Receipt uploaded successfully') (line 75), ErrorToast('Failed to upload receipt') (line 79) are all hardcoded. Component receives dictionary prop but does not use it for these strings.
fix: Use dictionary keys: const t = dictionary?.operator?.billing?.receipts; ErrorToast(t?.selectFile ?? 'Select a file') etc. Add keys to operator dictionary.
verdictReason: Confirmed at lines 59, 75, 79: all three toast messages are hardcoded English strings.

title: ThemeSelector uses dynamic require() for useTheme — violates React hooks rules
detail: const { theme, setTheme } = require('next-themes').useTheme() uses CommonJS require() inside a client component render function. This bypasses static module analysis and violates React's rules of hooks (hooks must be at the top level, not inside dynamic require). next-themes is already a project dependency with ESM support.
fix: Replace with a static import at the top of the file: import { useTheme } from 'next-themes' and call const { theme, setTheme } = useTheme() directly.
verdictReason: Confirmed at line 58: require('next-themes').useTheme() inside render body of a 'use client' component. No static import of useTheme exists in the file.

title: Dead code: lib/font.ts loads 6 Google Fonts but is never imported
detail: Loads Geist, Geist_Mono, Instrument_Sans, Noto_Sans_Mono, Mulish, and Inter via next/font/google. Every next/font load creates a bundle optimization step. No file in the codebase imports from saas-dashboard/lib/font (confirmed by grep — zero hits).
fix: Delete the file.
verdictReason: Confirmed: grep finds zero imports of saas-dashboard/lib/font anywhere in the codebase.

title: Dead code: all.tsx, featured.tsx, card.tsx never imported outside the tenants block
detail: AllTenants (all.tsx), FeaturedTenants/RecentTenants/TenantsRequiringAttention/TopTenantsByUsage (featured.tsx), and all card variants in card.tsx are never imported by any page, layout, or component outside this directory. A codebase-wide grep confirms zero imports of these components from outside the tenants block. types.ts exports Tenant/TenantWithOwners/TenantListItem and util.ts exports sortTenants/getTenantHealth/isBillingOverdue that are also only referenced by these dead files.
fix: Delete all.tsx and featured.tsx. Prune card.tsx to only what detail.tsx actually uses (none currently — detail.tsx is self-contained). Remove dead exports from types.ts and util.ts. This removes significant bundle and maintenance weight.
verdictReason: Codebase-wide grep confirms AllTenants, FeaturedTenants, TenantCard, TenantBillingCard, TenantCompactCard, TenantInfoCard, TenantStatsCard have zero imports outside all.tsx/featured.tsx/card.tsx themselves.

title: Duplicate tenantsSearchParams export — two independent cache instances for same URL params
detail: Both validation.ts and queries.ts independently export a tenantsSearchParams from createSearchParamsCache. The validation.ts version is more complete (8 keys including name, domain, planType, isActive, sort); queries.ts only has page/perPage/search (3 keys). If nuqs relies on a single cache instance per request, two distinct instances cause parse inconsistencies. Neither is currently imported by actual page code, but this is a latent source of subtle bugs.
fix: Delete tenantsSearchParams from queries.ts. Import it from validation.ts everywhere. The validation.ts version is the canonical one.
verdictReason: Both files confirmed to export tenantsSearchParams via createSearchParamsCache with different key sets. validation.ts:12 and queries.ts:16 verified directly.

title: growthRate metric shows new-tenant share, not period-over-period growth
detail: growthRate is computed as Math.round((recentSignups / totalTenants) * 100). This gives the percentage of the total tenant base that joined in the last 30 days — not a growth rate. A platform with 100 tenants and 5 new ones shows '+5%', which is misleading since the real growth rate requires comparing to the previous 30-day period.
fix: Add a second recentSignups count for the 30–60 day prior window and compute MoM: previousSignups > 0 ? Math.round(((recentSignups - previousSignups) / previousSignups) * 100) : 0. Alternatively rename the card to 'New This Month (%)' if true growth rate is not needed.
verdictReason: analysis.tsx lines 69-70 confirmed: growthRate = recentSignups / totalTenants * 100. This is new-tenant share, not MoM growth.

title: columns.tsx has two dead impersonation menu items pointing to non-existent /operator/ routes
detail: TenantActionsCell has: (1) <a href='/operator/tenants?impersonate={id}'> on line 276 — /operator path does not exist; (2) fetch to /operator/actions/impersonation/{id}/start on lines 219-230 — this route also does not exist. The working impersonation is tenantStartImpersonation server action already used in detail.tsx. Additionally, onToggleActive (lines 239-262) POSTs to /operator/actions/tenants/{id}/toggle-active — also a nonexistent route. Three actions in the dropdown menu are silently broken.
fix: Remove the href-based Impersonate link entirely. Replace onStartImpersonation and onToggleActive fetch calls with direct server action calls (tenantStartImpersonation, tenantToggleActive) as already done in detail.tsx.
verdictReason: columns.tsx lines 219-236, 239-261, 276 confirmed: three broken /operator/ fetches. No /operator/* routes exist in the app. Route listing shows only /api/admin/route.ts and the actual (saas-dashboard) routes.

title: invoices/route.ts shows amountPaid as invoice amount — open invoices appear as $0
detail: The invoices panel maps amount: i.amountPaid. For open/unpaid invoices (amountDue=10000, amountPaid=0), the operator sees $0.00 — misleading them into thinking nothing is owed. Only paid invoices (amountPaid = amountDue) display correctly.
fix: Change to amount: i.amountDue. If partial payment visibility is needed, expose both fields: { amountDue: i.amountDue, amountPaid: i.amountPaid } and update TenantDetail table columns accordingly.
verdictReason: invoices/route.ts line 28 confirmed: amount: i.amountPaid. Invoice model has amountDue and amountPaid as separate Int fields. For any open invoice, amountPaid will be 0.

title: School model missing @@index on isActive and planType — full table scans on analysis page
detail: School model has no @@index for isActive or planType. analysis.tsx runs multiple db.school.count({ where: { isActive: true|false } }) and db.school.count({ where: { planType: 'X' } }) queries, plus the main tenant list filters by both. Without indexes these are sequential scans. createdAt is also used for orderBy on the main list with no index.
fix: Add @@index([isActive]), @@index([planType]), @@index([createdAt]) to the School model. Apply via Neon MCP ALTER TABLE ... ADD INDEX and record in schema. No data loss risk.
verdictReason: school.prisma confirmed: School model ends with only @@map('schools') at line 424. No @@index on isActive, planType, or createdAt. Only @unique constraints exist (domain, joinCode, createdByUserId).

title: Hardcoded English column headers, cell strings and filter labels in columns.tsx
detail: All column headers ('Name', 'Subdomain', 'Plan', 'Users', 'Catalog', 'Status', 'Created', 'Trial Ends'), cell strings ('students', 'teachers', 'levels, grades', 'Not configured', 'Active', 'Inactive'), filter option labels ('Trial', 'Basic', 'Premium', 'Enterprise', 'Active', 'Inactive'), and 'Actions' are hardcoded English. getTenantColumns() accepts no dictionary argument.
fix: Add dictionary?: OperatorDictionary parameter to getTenantColumns(). Pass it from TenantsTable which receives initialData from the server component that already has the dictionary. Replace all hardcoded strings with dictionary?.operator?.tenants?.columns.* keys and add keys to operator-ar.json / operator-en.json.
verdictReason: columns.tsx confirmed: getTenantColumns() signature has no dictionary parameter. All string literals verified at lines 52-204.

title: Hardcoded English strings in delete-dialog.tsx and table.tsx grid view
detail: delete-dialog.tsx hardcodes 'Delete Tenant', 'All data will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone.', 'Cancel', 'Deleting...', 'Delete School'. table.tsx hardcodes 'No schools found', 'Schools will appear here once they complete onboarding.', 'Search schools...', 'Impersonation active — actions disabled', 'Loading...', 'Load More'. Neither receives a dictionary prop.
fix: Pass dictionary from page.tsx through TenantsContent → TenantsTable → client components. Use dictionary?.operator?.tenants keys. Add missing keys to operator-en.json and operator-ar.json.
verdictReason: delete-dialog.tsx lines 79-112 and table.tsx lines 125-176 confirmed: all listed strings are hardcoded with no dictionary prop on either component.

title: content.tsx status filter checks 'active' but DataTable column emits 'true' — filter never applies
detail: content.tsx filters by searchParams.status === 'active' (line 32). columns.tsx status column options use { label: 'Active', value: 'true' } / { label: 'Inactive', value: 'false' } (lines 158-161). When the DataTable column filter is applied, status='true' appears in the URL. content.tsx then evaluates 'true' === 'active' → false, so the isActive filter is silently dropped on SSR. queries.ts handles 'true'/'false' correctly, but content.tsx initial render does not.
fix: Align the filter value format in one of two ways: (a) update content.tsx to handle status === 'true' and status === 'false' alongside the legacy 'active'/'inactive' values, or (b) update columns.tsx filter options to { value: 'active' } / { value: 'inactive' } and content.tsx to match. Option (b) is cleaner and removes the string/boolean dual-format.
verdictReason: content.tsx line 32: isActive: searchParams.status === 'active'. columns.tsx lines 158-161: options with value 'true'/'false'. The two strings never match — status filter on SSR render is permanently broken.

title: FileUploader imported but unused in receipts/content.tsx
detail: import { FileUploader } from '@/components/saas-dashboard/file-uploader' at line 18 is present but FileUploader is never referenced anywhere in the component. This is a leftover from a draft that was replaced by ReceiptUpload in upload.tsx.
fix: Remove the unused import from line 18.
verdictReason: Confirmed: receipts/content.tsx line 18 imports FileUploader from saas-dashboard/file-uploader but no JSX in the file references FileUploader.

title: Two parallel CSV export paths cause UX confusion and code duplication
detail: InvoicesTable at line 84-90 renders an 'Export CSV' button via exportTableToCSV (client-side, exports only currently loaded rows). BillingContent also renders an ExportButton (calls getInvoicesCSV server action, exports up to 10,000 rows). Two export buttons with different behaviors appear in the UI with no explanation of the difference.
fix: Remove the exportTableToCSV button from InvoicesTable's DataTableToolbar. Keep only the ExportButton (server-action-based) which correctly exports all filtered matches. Optionally relabel it 'Export all (CSV)' for clarity.
verdictReason: Confirmed: table.tsx lines 83-91 render an 'Export CSV' button using client-side exportTableToCSV; content.tsx lines 225-232 also render an ExportButton. Both appear in the billing invoices tab simultaneously.

title: Redundant findUnique before update/delete in updateQuestion, deleteQuestion, updateAssignment, deleteAssignment
detail: updateQuestion (line 68) fetches db.question.findUnique solely to check existence, then calls db.question.update. deleteQuestion does the same (lines 116-118). updateAssignment and deleteAssignment in assignment-actions.ts have the same pattern (lines 61-62, 99-100). Since Prisma update throws P2025 RecordNotFound when the record does not exist, the findUnique is redundant — four extra round-trips per write operation.
fix: Remove the findUnique pre-check. Catch the Prisma P2025 error in the catch block and map it to { success: false, error: 'question_not_found' }. This eliminates the extra DB call while preserving the error response.
verdictReason: Directly verified all four functions: each does findUnique purely for existence check immediately before an update/delete that would throw P2025 anyway.

title: approvalStatus included in Zod update schema for Question — setting it to undefined relies on Prisma undefined-skip behavior
detail: catalogQuestionSchema includes approvalStatus as a parseable field. In updateQuestion (question-actions.ts line 97), the field is suppressed via approvalStatus: undefined in the update spread. Setting undefined in a Prisma update payload means 'do not update this field', which works but is fragile and relies on Prisma's undefined-skip behavior rather than explicit exclusion. By contrast, updateAssignment uses the correct pattern: explicit destructure { approvalStatus, visibility, status, ...safeData } = validated then passes only safeData.
fix: Remove approvalStatus, visibility, and status from catalogQuestionSchema entirely (they are server-controlled). In updateQuestion, use the same pattern as updateAssignment: const { approvalStatus, visibility, status, ...safeData } = validated; await db.question.update({ where: { id }, data: safeData }).
verdictReason: Directly verified: question-validation.ts includes approvalStatus in the schema; question-actions.ts updateQuestion uses approvalStatus: undefined spread (fragile) rather than the destructure pattern used correctly in assignment-actions.ts.

title: Approval-table 'Contributed By' column renders raw userId string — no name resolution
detail: The 'Contributed By' column (line 205) renders userId || 'System' — a raw CUID string to the operator, not a human-readable name. Video rows join the user for username/email (approval-content.tsx lines 108-113 via user relation) but for Question/Material/Assignment/Book rows only the raw contributedBy (userId string) is fetched with no user name join.
fix: In approval-content.tsx, after building the pending arrays, batch-fetch user display names: db.user.findMany({ where: { id: { in: contributorIds } }, select: { id: true, email: true, username: true } }) and build a map. Pass the resolved name into each PendingItem. This is a single extra query for all pending items.
verdictReason: Directly verified: approval-table.tsx line 205 renders userId directly; approval-content.tsx only joins user for Video rows, not Question/Material/Assignment/Book.

title: deleteBook / updateBook fire a redundant findUnique before delete/update
detail: deleteBook (lines 109-131) fetches the row with findUnique to confirm it exists, then deletes it — two sequential queries. updateBook (lines 60-107) similarly fetches the existing row before updating. If the row doesn't exist, Prisma's delete/update throws P2025 which the outer catch already handles. The pre-flight findUnique adds a round trip for every delete/update operation.
fix: Remove the preflight findUnique calls. Catch Prisma.PrismaClientKnownRequestError with code 'P2025' in the catch block and map it to the appropriate error code ('catalog_book_not_found'). This halves the DB round trips for delete/update operations.
verdictReason: updateBook lines 67-70 and deleteBook lines 113-116 both confirmed: findUnique preflight before the actual update/delete, with the catch block already in place to handle errors.

title: CatalogContent and AnalyticsContent declare a dictionary prop but never use it
detail: CatalogContent's Props interface (lines 12-14) includes dictionary but the function destructures only { lang } (line 17). AnalyticsContent (analytics-content.tsx lines 34-36, 39) has the same issue. The dictionary is fetched and serialized by each page.tsx and passed down unnecessarily — wasted getDictionary() call and serialization overhead on every request.
fix: For CatalogContent: remove dictionary from Props interface and stop passing it from catalog/page.tsx. For AnalyticsContent: either remove it (short term) or properly use it to translate all the hardcoded card labels.
verdictReason: content.tsx Props interface at lines 12-14 declares dictionary; function at line 17 destructures only { lang }. analytics-content.tsx Props at lines 34-36 declares dictionary; function at line 39 destructures only { lang }. Both confirmed unused.

title: Skeleton files (bar-graph-skeleton, pie-graph-skeleton, area-graph-skeleton, recent-sales-skeleton) are never imported — dead code
detail: All four skeleton files export components that are never imported by any other file in the codebase. They were prepared for Suspense streaming but the streaming boundary was never wired. They add dead bundle weight.
fix: Either wire them as Suspense fallbacks in a proper streaming layout or delete them. Given charts currently render hardcoded data synchronously, deletion is the shorter path.
verdictReason: Grep for BarGraphSkeleton, PieGraphSkeleton, AreaGraphSkeleton, RecentSalesSkeleton confirmed zero imports outside their own definition files.

title: content.tsx runs 5 parallel DB queries on every request with no caching
detail: Four count queries (db.school.count × 2, db.user.count, db.student.count) plus getWeatherData run on every operator page visit with no unstable_cache or React cache() wrapper. These cross-tenant aggregates change infrequently (only on school onboarding).
fix: Wrap the four counts in unstable_cache with a 5-minute revalidate and tags ['schools','users','students']. Invalidate on school creation/deletion via revalidateTag.
verdictReason: Promise.all at content.tsx lines 21-27 confirmed with no caching wrapper; four uncached db.*.count() calls confirmed running on every page load.

title: task-card.tsx renders hardcoded 'Task' badge with no i18n
detail: Confirmed: line 84 renders <Badge variant='outline' className='ms-auto font-semibold'>Task</Badge>. TaskCard receives no dictionary prop and has no i18n fallback for this badge label.
fix: Pass dictionary down from KanbanBoard → TaskCard and replace 'Task' with a dictionary key (e.g. dictionary?.operator?.kanban?.taskBadge ?? 'Task').
verdictReason: Line 84 confirmed as hardcoded string; TaskCard interface has no dictionary prop.

title: products/components/product-tables/options.tsx CATEGORY_OPTIONS has zero consumers and uses e-commerce categories irrelevant to school SaaS
detail: Confirmed: CATEGORY_OPTIONS (line 21, tagged @deprecated) has exactly one occurrence in the codebase — its own declaration. No file imports it. The category list (Electronics, Furniture, Clothing, Toys, Groceries, Books, Jewelry, Beauty Products) is a generic e-commerce scaffold with zero relevance to a school SaaS operator dashboard.
fix: Delete options.tsx along with the rest of the dead products/components tree (columns.tsx stub, cell-action.tsx stub, index.tsx stub, product-listing.tsx, product-view-page.tsx). If Products is ever revived, create domain-appropriate categories (e.g. STARTER_PLAN, PRO_PLAN, ENTERPRISE_PLAN, ADD_ON).
verdictReason: grep confirms CATEGORY_OPTIONS is declared but imported by zero other files; categories are confirmed as e-commerce scaffold irrelevant to the domain.

title: kanban/page.tsx PageNav groups Kanban with /dashboard and /analytics — confusing cross-feature IA
detail: Confirmed: kanbanPages (lines 25-29) contains Overview → /dashboard, Analytics → /analytics, Kanban → /kanban. This renders a three-tab nav bar at the top of the kanban page linking to entirely different sections of the app. Every other saas-dashboard route (sales, tenants) uses PageNav only for sub-routes within the same section. The pattern makes the nav bar misleading and is inconsistent with the rest of the IA.
fix: If kanban is kept, remove the cross-linking PageNav and either omit it entirely or create kanban-specific sub-routes (/kanban/board, /kanban/backlog). If kanban is removed from nav, delete the page.
verdictReason: Lines 25-29 of kanban/page.tsx confirmed; PageNav links to /dashboard and /analytics from within the /kanban page, inconsistent with all other saas-dashboard pages.

title: dnsConfigDescription renders the count twice when dictionary key is present
detail: The JSX renders {stats.approvedRequests}{' '} on line 261, then on line 263 replaces {count} in the dictionary string (which is '{count} domain(s) are approved...') with the same number. Result when dictionary is present: '3 3 domain(s) are approved and waiting for DNS configuration'. The finding's stated failure mode ('count silently omitted') is inverted — the actual bug is the count appearing twice.
fix: Remove the standalone {stats.approvedRequests}{' '} JSX node (line 261) and rely solely on the dictionary replace: {t?.dnsConfigDescription?.replace('{count}', String(stats.approvedRequests)) ?? `${stats.approvedRequests} domain(s) are approved and waiting for DNS configuration`}
verdictReason: The bug is real (double-count when dict key present) but the original finding misdescribed the failure mode as 'count omitted'. The actual output is count rendered twice. Fix is to remove the standalone {stats.approvedRequests} node.

title: Approval Rate stat conflates 'approved' (pending DNS) with 'verified' (fully live) — misleading operator metric
detail: approvalRate = (approved + verified) / total. 'Approved' means the operator said yes but DNS is not yet configured — it is transient. In a steady state with many approved-but-not-configured domains, the metric reads misleadingly high. The stat card label says 'Approval Rate' which operators reasonably interpret as 'working domains'.
fix: Either (a) rename to 'Acceptance Rate' and add a tooltip distinguishing approved vs verified, or (b) split into two metrics: Acceptance Rate = (approved+verified)/total and Verification Rate = verified/(approved+verified).
verdictReason: Verified at content.tsx lines 118-124: approvalRate formula includes both approved and verified counts; stat card at line 209 shows it as 'Approval Rate' with no distinction.

title: COMMON_ACTIONS imported but never used in util.ts — unused import
detail: COMMON_ACTIONS is listed in the import from './config' on line 12 but does not appear in any function body in util.ts. Only ACTION_CATEGORY_LABELS, DATE_RANGE_PRESETS, LOG_LEVEL_LABELS, and PERFORMANCE_THRESHOLDS are actually used. This triggers lint warnings and adds confusion.
fix: Remove COMMON_ACTIONS from the import list on line 12.
verdictReason: Grep confirms COMMON_ACTIONS appears only on line 12 (import) and nowhere else in util.ts.

title: exportLogsToCSV: CSV column headers are hardcoded English
detail: The CSV export function hardcodes English column headers: Timestamp, Action, User, Tenant, IP, Level, Request ID, Reason. When the operator dashboard is in Arabic locale, downloaded CSVs will always have English headers.
fix: Accept an optional headers array parameter: exportLogsToCSV(logs, { headers?: string[] }). Pass dictionary-translated column names from the caller.
verdictReason: util.ts lines 297-306 confirmed hardcoded English headers array with no i18n parameter.

title: conference/page.tsx reuses observability.title key — both Observability and Conference Observability pages show identical heading
detail: conference/page.tsx uses PageHeadingSetter title={d?.observability?.title || 'Observability'} — the same key as the main observability page. Both pages display 'Observability' as the heading, making it impossible to distinguish them by title. A separate operator.observability.conference.title key should be used.
fix: Add a conference-specific title key to both operator-en.json and operator-ar.json under operator.observability.conference.title (e.g., 'Conference Observability' / 'مراقبة المؤتمرات'). Update conference/page.tsx to use d?.observability?.conference?.title || 'Conference Observability'.
verdictReason: The original finding describes a missing dictionary key but both en and ar dictionaries already have observability.title; the actual bug is conference/page.tsx reusing that key instead of a conference-specific one.

title: Perf/DX: dueBeforeFor() called twice per render in table filter construction
detail: Line 129: ...(dueBeforeFor(due) ? { dueBefore: dueBeforeFor(due) } : {}) calls dueBeforeFor(due) twice. dueBeforeFor calls new Date() internally so the two calls may return different millisecond timestamps; the double-call is wasteful and inelegant.
fix: Cache: const dueBefore = dueBeforeFor(due); const filters = { ..., ...(dueBefore ? { dueBefore } : {}) }
verdictReason: Confirmed at line 129: dueBeforeFor(due) is called twice in the same expression.

title: Perf: getOperatorLeadById fetches all Lead columns with no select clause
detail: db.lead.findFirst({ where: ... }) with no select fetches all columns including emailVerified, phoneVerified, importId, importedAt, metadata (Json), and assignedToId — none of which are in the OperatorLeadDetail return type. The caller manually picks only the needed fields.
fix: Add an explicit select clause mirroring the OperatorLeadDetail type fields to eliminate the ~6 unused columns from the wire payload.
verdictReason: Confirmed: line 403 findFirst has no select clause; Lead model has emailVerified, phoneVerified, importId, importedAt, metadata, assignedToId fields not in the return type.

title: Perf: getOperatorLeadActivities fires a redundant lead existence check when called in parallel with getOperatorLeadById
detail: getOperatorLeadActivities does a db.lead.findFirst to confirm lead existence (select id only), then immediately does db.leadActivity.findMany. The page at sales/[id]/page.tsx calls both getOperatorLeadById and getOperatorLeadActivities in Promise.all — so the existence pre-check in getOperatorLeadActivities duplicates a fetch that getOperatorLeadById already performs, adding an extra sequential DB round-trip within getOperatorLeadActivities.
fix: Remove the existence pre-check in getOperatorLeadActivities. Rely on the schoolId filter on the activity query; return empty array if no activities exist. If the security justification (preventing activity from real schools surfacing) is important, note that LeadActivity.schoolId=PLATFORM_SCHOOL_ID in the findMany where already provides that guarantee.
verdictReason: Confirmed: lines 543-549 do a lead findFirst before the activity findMany; sales/[id]/page.tsx confirms both functions are called in Promise.all, making the inner existence check redundant.

title: Security: error messages return raw Prisma exception strings to client
detail: All catch blocks return error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'fallback', exposing raw Prisma constraint error messages (e.g. 'Unique constraint failed on the constraint: leads_schoolId_email_key') to the DEVELOPER client. Also violates the error-code pattern from translation.md.
fix: Return structured error codes (errorCode: 'DUPLICATE_EMAIL', errorCode: 'LEAD_NOT_FOUND') and map them to dictionary strings on the client per the translation.md error-code pattern.
verdictReason: Confirmed at lines 112, 210, 245: raw error.message exposed. Risk is low (DEVELOPER-only dashboard) but violates the project's own error-code convention.

title: Dead code: duplicate use-callback-ref.tsx + unused hooks (use-mobile, use-media-query, use-multistep-form, use-controllable-state)
detail: use-callback-ref.tsx is identical to use-callback-ref.ts (diff shows no difference). use-mobile.tsx, use-media-query.ts, use-multistep-form.tsx, and use-controllable-state.tsx have zero imports outside the saas-dashboard/hooks/ directory itself. These are scaffold hooks copied from a starter template.
fix: Delete use-callback-ref.tsx (keep .ts). Delete use-mobile.tsx, use-media-query.ts, use-multistep-form.tsx, use-controllable-state.tsx.
verdictReason: Confirmed: diff between .ts and .tsx shows no differences; grep finds zero imports of these hooks outside the hooks/ directory itself.

title: Dead code: 4 skeleton components are never imported and bar-graph-skeleton uses Math.random causing hydration mismatch
detail: BarGraphSkeleton, AreaGraphSkeleton, PieGraphSkeleton, and RecentSalesSkeleton are each defined but never imported (confirmed: grep finds no imports outside their own files). bar-graph-skeleton.tsx:35 uses Math.random() to generate skeleton bar heights — this causes hydration mismatch when SSR and client render different heights.
fix: Delete all 4 files. Use fixed skeleton heights (e.g. fixed percentages or Skeleton from @/components/ui/skeleton with static sizes) if chart placeholders are needed.
verdictReason: Confirmed: grep finds zero imports of all 4 skeleton components outside their own files; Math.random() at bar-graph-skeleton.tsx:35 confirmed.

title: Dead code: app-sidebar.tsx and org-switcher.tsx are unused scaffold stubs with invalid routes
detail: AppSidebar is not imported anywhere (confirmed by grep). It uses /operator/* paths that do not exist as routes (production routes are under /(saas-dashboard)/). OrgSwitcher (org-switcher.tsx) is also not imported anywhere. Both are dead scaffold.
fix: Delete both files.
verdictReason: Confirmed: grep finds zero imports of AppSidebar or OrgSwitcher outside their own files; navItems reference /operator/* non-existent paths.

title: Dead code: lib/utils.ts duplicates @/lib/utils — never imported
detail: saas-dashboard/lib/utils.ts exports cn() and formatBytes() which are already in @/lib/utils. All files within saas-dashboard import directly from @/lib/utils — none import from saas-dashboard/lib/utils (confirmed by grep). NOTE: lib/tenant.ts is NOT dead — it is actively imported by 13 API route files as a backward-compat re-export, so that part of the original finding is incorrect.
fix: Delete saas-dashboard/lib/utils.ts only. Keep lib/tenant.ts.
verdictReason: lib/utils.ts confirmed dead (zero external imports). lib/tenant.ts is NOT dead — confirmed 13 API route files import getTenantContext from it. Finding adjusted to exclude tenant.ts.

title: Hardcoded pricing badge strings in analysis.tsx plan distribution cards
detail: The plan distribution cards display <Badge>Free</Badge>, <Badge>$99/mo</Badge>, <Badge>$299/mo</Badge>, <Badge>Custom</Badge> as hardcoded English strings with no dictionary key. If pricing changes, a code edit is required. The dictionary system is already in use in this file (t?.trial, t?.basic etc.) but these badge labels are bypassing it.
fix: Move plan pricing labels to operator-en.json / operator-ar.json under operator.tenants.plans.{trial,basic,premium,enterprise}.badge. Replace Badge children with dictionary lookups.
verdictReason: analysis.tsx lines 169/184/199/214 confirmed: hardcoded 'Free', '$99/mo', '$299/mo', 'Custom' as Badge children with no dictionary reference.

title: detail.tsx dictionary prop typed as any — no compile-time safety for key access
detail: TenantDetailProps declares dictionary?: any. Every dictionary access (d?.impersonationStarted, d?.planChanged, etc.) is unchecked — typos produce undefined at runtime with no compile-time error. search.tsx has the same pattern.
fix: Import and use Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getDictionary>> as the dictionary prop type (same as content.tsx and analysis.tsx). TypeScript will then catch key mismatches at compile time.
verdictReason: detail.tsx line 32 confirmed: dictionary?: any. content.tsx and analysis.tsx correctly use Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getDictionary>>.
